name: History Facts
subject: The Broadway musical that started it all
body:
On September 12, 1866, a packed house at Niblo's Garden theater at the junction of Broadway and Prince Street in New York City witnessed a unique spectacle with the six-plus-hour debut of The Black Crook. [](https://track.historyfacts.com/?xtl=1krv9cfc68cfk6pjcnb328bg3x1yar1zaco70wzwcu2gwttfhr0o0socux084hdyzrnaql6gd92ubxlkd75r44dd1j9haww55be3xt40t0uurah3joweeog03wgs7fm5ulj1zuyz32bb8sg7jod4dwm5w&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sq4puf&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
---
---
# [The first Broadway musical was performed in 1866.](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sq4puf&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
### [Arts & Culture](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sq4puf&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sq4puf&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
| O n September 12, 1866, a packed house at Niblo's Garden theater at the junction of Broadway and Prince Street in New York City witnessed a unique spectacle with the six-plus-hour debut of _The Black Crook_. Dozens of dancers in flesh-colored tights twirled around the stage as glittering fairies; ominous lighting and noises announced the onset of a hurricane; and the banter between the main characters was interrupted by such plucky songs as "You Naughty, Naughty Men." Although this was hardly the first stage performance to incorporate singing into the drama, the explosive combination of music, dancing, and elaborate set theatrics led _The Black Crook_ to be widely recognized as the starting point for the [Broadway musical](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sq4puf&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com).
---
The creation of the play was something of an accident. A Parisian ballet troupe had been booked for performances at Manhattan's Academy of Music, but was left without a venue when the opera house was destroyed in a May 1866 fire. Its producers subsequently struck a deal with Niblo's Garden manager William Wheatley, who determined that the troupe's attractive performers and expensive set machinery would liven up Charles M. Barras’ original script for _The Black Crook_ , a Faustian drama about a poor artist’s entanglements with an evil count and an agent of the devil.
While the visual effects helped divert attention from the shaky plot, it was the scantily clad dancers who took up most of the ink in reviews and ignited the largest [furor](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sq4puf&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) among moralists who condemned the indecent display of flesh. But the indignation only served to heighten interest: _The Black Crook_ continued for nearly 500 performances in its first run, grossed more than $1 million, and spawned a sequel called _The White Fawn_ , all of which demonstrated to the industry that this musical extravaganza was very much a model worth imitating.
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sq4puf&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) | [](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sq4puf&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)

## "Moose Murders" is considered the nadir of Broadway flops.
If _The Black Crook_ was the happy result of an accident, then the notorious _Moose Murders_ represents the train wreck that can ensue when a production fails to gel into something resembling coherency. Penned by young playwright Arthur Bicknell, the 1983 play was [intended](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sq4puf&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) to be a farcical murder mystery about a vacationing family and the strange inhabitants they encounter at an Adirondack lodge. However, things headed south when the script wound up in the possession of a first-time director, whose wife was both bankrolling and appearing in the show.

The aging big-name star, Eve Arden, left after one preview due to an inability to remember her lines. While her replacement, Holland Taylor, quickly got up to speed, her professionalism couldn't offset the confusion created by such characters as a Native American caretaker with an Irish accent and a man-eating moose. At the conclusion of what turned out to be the only official performance on February 22, 1983, critic Brendan Gill summed up the disaster by noting it "would insult the intelligence of an audience consisting entirely of amoebas." Still, there was something to be salvaged from a production that became shorthand for a spectacular failure: _Moose Murders_ occasionally enjoys a revival from a director and cast that leans into the weirdness, while Bicknell turned his painful experience into a memoir titled _Moose Murdered, or How I Learned To Stop Worrying and Love My Broadway Bomb_.

clean_body: On September 12, 1866, a packed house at Niblo's Garden theater at the junction of Broadway and Prince Street in New York City witnessed a unique spectacle with the six-plus-hour debut of The Black Crook. | O n September 12, 1866, a packed house at Niblo's Garden theater at the junction of Broadway and Prince Street in New York City witnessed a unique spectacle with the six-plus-hour debut of The Black Crook. Dozens of dancers in flesh-colored tights twirled around the stage as glittering fairies; ominous lighting and noises announced the onset of a hurricane; and the banter between the main characters was interrupted by such plucky songs as "You Naughty, Naughty Men." Although this was hardly the first stage performance to incorporate singing into the drama, the explosive combination of music, dancing, and elaborate set theatrics led The Black Crook to be widely recognized as the starting point for the . The creation of the play was something of an accident. A Parisian ballet troupe had been booked for performances at Manhattan's Academy of Music, but was left without a venue when the opera house was destroyed in a May 1866 fire. Its producers subsequently struck a deal with Niblo's Garden manager William Wheatley, who determined that the troupe's attractive performers and expensive set machinery would liven up Charles M. Barras’ original script for The Black Crook , a Faustian drama about a poor artist’s entanglements with an evil count and an agent of the devil. While the visual effects helped divert attention from the shaky plot, it was the scantily clad dancers who took up most of the ink in reviews and ignited the largest among moralists who condemned the indecent display of flesh. But the indignation only served to heighten interest: The Black Crook continued for nearly 500 performances in its first run, grossed more than $1 million, and spawned a sequel called The White Fawn , all of which demonstrated to the industry that this musical extravaganza was very much a model worth imitating. | Thank you for supporting our advertisers! | By the Numbers | | Year The Black Crook takes place 1600 | | Operational Broadway theaters in New York 41 | Operational Broadway theaters in New York 41 | | Broadway performances of The Phantom of the Opera , a record 13,981 | | Minimum seating capacity for most official Broadway theaters 500 | Minimum seating capacity for most official Broadway theaters 500 | | | Did you know? ---|---|--- "Moose Murders" is considered the nadir of Broadway flops. If The Black Crook was the happy result of an accident, then the notorious Moose Murders represents the train wreck that can ensue when a production fails to gel into something resembling coherency. Penned by young playwright Arthur Bicknell, the 1983 play was to be a farcical murder mystery about a vacationing family and the strange inhabitants they encounter at an Adirondack lodge. However, things headed south when the script wound up in the possession of a first-time director, whose wife was both bankrolling and appearing in the show. The aging big-name star, Eve Arden, left after one preview due to an inability to remember her lines. While her replacement, Holland Taylor, quickly got up to speed, her professionalism couldn't offset the confusion created by such characters as a Native American caretaker with an Irish accent and a man-eating moose. At the conclusion of what turned out to be the only official performance on February 22, 1983, critic Brendan Gill summed up the disaster by noting it "would insult the intelligence of an audience consisting entirely of amoebas." Still, there was something to be salvaged from a production that became shorthand for a spectacular failure: Moose Murders occasionally enjoys a revival from a director and cast that leans into the weirdness, while Bicknell turned his painful experience into a memoir titled Moose Murdered, or How I Learned To Stop Worrying and Love My Broadway Bomb. name: No Dem Left Behind PAC
subject: Bondi & Rubio: Trump's latest grab for power.
body:
Today's confirmation hearings are just a continuation of the grab for power Trump has demonstrated since November 5th.
| | | | | | [](https://click.nodemleftbehind.com/click/gpnv-2w36w4-t5a1su-kyd76rh7/)
---
# **Bondi & Rubio: Trump's latest grab for power.**
---
| [](https://click.nodemleftbehind.com/click/gpnv-2w36w4-t5a1sv-kyd76rh8/)
---
| | | | Hello friend, Trump’s nominations of Pam Bondi and Marco Rubio are a direct assault on justice and diplomacy. Their records are proof of the corruption and chaos they’ll bring to our government. Trump is not filling these government positions with people who want to serve our country, he is using the Senate confirmation hearings as a grab for power.

### **Pam Bondi: Turning Justice Into a Weapon**
Bondi's nomination as Attorney General is like asking an arsonist to investigate the fire. Don’t let corporate media rewrite history—she defended Trump during his impeachment. Winning in 2024 doesn’t erase his 2020 insurrection. Bondi is not fit to serve; she’s a threat to democracy. She has built a career around being a Trump loyalist, plain and simple. She pushed lies about voter fraud in the 2020 election, has threatened Trump’s political enemies, and used her power for personal gain—remember the $25,000 Trump donation that made her drop the Trump University case? As Attorney General, she’d turn the Justice Department into a tool for revenge and corruption, shredding democracy in the process.

### **Marco Rubio: A Warmonger at State**
Marco Rubio’s hawkish foreign policy is reckless and dangerous. From backing the Iraq War to pushing for regime change in Venezuela, he’s consistently chosen war over diplomacy. As Secretary of State, his opposition to peace deals and preference for conflict could drag us into unnecessary global entanglements. With Rubio in this role, Trump’s dangerous imperialist fantasies could become reality. Greenland up for grabs? The Panama Canal under threat? MAGA’s bizarre conquests are not far-fetched with a war hawk like Rubio steering U.S. foreign policy. The world cannot afford his recklessness.

### **Stop This Power Grab**
Every single MAGA confirmation hearing so far has been a train wreck, exposing candidates who are blatantly unfit, dangerously unqualified, and outright threats to our country. Pam Bondi and Marco Rubio are the worst of the worst.
### Bondi’s loyalty to Trump and history of corruption would turn the Justice Department into his personal weapon, while Rubio’s hawkish foreign policy and devotion to Trump’s agenda could destabilize global relations. These two aren’t just bad choices—they’re a direct path to giving Trump unchecked power over our government.
[**_We need to call out these dangerous nominations while we can. Each of Trump's picks threaten our country’s integrity and global standing. Their nominations must be stopped. Join us in fighting back against Trump’s corrupt, chaotic agenda._**](https://click.nodemleftbehind.com/click/gpnv-2w36w4-t5a1sw-kyd76rh9/)

clean_body: The Democrats are already plotting to steal Elise Stefanik's seat - will you let them get away with it? | Fellow Conservative, **** As President Trump's newly appointed UN Ambassador, her departure leaves a crucial seat up for grabs. They're prepared to do, say, and spend whatever it takes to defeat us. But we can't let them succeed. The stakes are high, and the consequences of losing this seat are dire. We must act now to ensure that our values and principles are represented in Congress. | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100413067/522698826/-793144256?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDIzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMzMxMmU3Mi0zY2QzLWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GuhqAUGhpHZNtLxaAP1fyZ14fCeV-FM5j3msU4v_7ng=&emci=9c064358-e1d1-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&emdi=23312e72-3cd3-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&ceid=15759664)

Hey there Derek, As we kick off 2025, we want to be transparent about the ambitious goals our team has set to build on last year’s successes and fight for a brighter future in Virginia. **Every department of Team DPVA is laser-focused on delivering results** that will energize voters, strengthen our democracy, and keep Virginia blue. Here’s what we’re aiming to accomplish this year:
* **Shyam Raman, Executive Director:** _Reach 500,000 voters before Election Day_ through grassroots organizing and outreach.
* **Vicki Daguerre-Bradford, Finance Director:**_Increase fundraising by 10% before the 3rd quarter,_ giving our team the resources we need to win.
* **Kelsey Carolan, Communications Director:** _Expand our social media reach by 120% by Election Day_ to engage voters online like never before.
* **Hannah Wheaton, Deputy Finance Director:** _Raise $300,000 from fundraising events throughout the year_ to fuel critical campaign operations

This is a team effort, and we can’t reach these goals without you. Your support powers every voter we contact, every campaign event we host, and every ad we run. [_**That’s why we’re asking for your support in a big way by becoming a recurring donor. A monthly contribution – even just $10 – helps us plan ahead, sustain our work, and ensure we have the resources to succeed. Will you commit to a monthly donation to help us achieve these ambitious goals?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100413068/522698827/-1953633157?&recurring=auto&refcode=E11955-20250115-Goals-SL-Other&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDIzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMzMxMmU3Mi0zY2QzLWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GuhqAUGhpHZNtLxaAP1fyZ14fCeV-FM5j3msU4v_7ng=) | **************[BECOME A MONTHLY DONOR](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100413069/522698828/-1953633157?&recurring=auto&refcode=E11955-20250115-Goals-SB-Other&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDIzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMzMxMmU3Mi0zY2QzLWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GuhqAUGhpHZNtLxaAP1fyZ14fCeV-FM5j3msU4v_7ng=)**************

name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: The Mountain Minute: 1/15/2025
body:
Rep. Winter asks Coloradans to ‘get out to the stock show’ and see the state’s $47 billion industry
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**PRESENTED BY[ROCKYMOUNTAINVOICE.COM](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
| | Hey everyone,
Here is a summary of today's newsletter:
🏛️ **Colorado Legislative Session Updates...** As Democrats push to undo it with Senate Bill 25-005, Independence Institute president Jon Caldara has filed a ballot initiative to counter the bill and [make Colorado a “right-to-work” state](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), ensuring employees can opt out of unions and dues. Also, discussions on [Colorado's $47 billion agriculture industry](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) also took center stage, with representatives encouraging residents to engage with the sector at the stock show.
**🏔️ Here in Colorado...** Grand Junction City Council candidate [Ben Van Dyke](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) advocates for prioritizing community needs over special interests. Meanwhile, [declining enrollment](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) in Colorado schools is straining budgets statewide, forcing districts to confront tough choices about resource allocation. Additionally, Cory Gaines shows Denver continuous battle with homelessness and safety concerns, with over [$24 million spent in 2024](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) on homelessness programs.
🇺🇸**On the National Stage...** _Not featured below, but for your personal reading:_ Two House Democrats joined Republicans in supporting a bill to [ban biological males from competing in girls' sports](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.). Pete Hegseth faces a [smear campaign](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), but veterans rally in his defense, marching in Washington, D.C. Meanwhile, [Carrie Underwood](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) is set to perform "America the Beautiful" at the upcoming inauguration.
🗣️**Commentary Spotlight...** Greg Walcher reflects on the [legacy of Jimmy Carter](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), criticizing policies that walled off significant portions of Alaska from development, negatively impacting rural economies. Patricia McMahon delivers a [major critique of Denver's safety issues](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) under Mayor Johnston, highlighting firsthand experiences of crime and inadequate responses.
This is the Mountain Minute for Wednesday, January 15, 2025.
– Heidi Ganahl
---
|
---
**TODAY'S TOP STORIES**
**[Rep. Winter asks Coloradans to ‘get out to the stock show’ and see the state’s $47 billion industry](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY BRIAN PORTER, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
A leading voice of agriculture in the Colorado House, Rep. Ty Winter reminded urban and rural legislators alike Tuesday of the National Western Stock Show in Denver.
“Its stock show week here in Denver,” said Winter, the assistant minority leader and representative for nine Southeastern Colorado counties.
The 119th edition of the National Western Stock Show runs through Jan. 26 with a variety of livestock exhibitions, rodeos and more.
“Please get out and see your ag producers,” said Winter, in an announcement from the floor of the House. “See what they do to make sure you have food on the table and clothes on your back.”
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support!
[DONATE TO ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
|
---
**[Ben Van Dyke wants to be voice of reason and of the people on Grand Junction City Council](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Ben Van Dyke, a fourth-generation Grand Junction resident, is running for City Council to prioritize community needs, particularly affordable housing, public safety, and reversing controversial street changes.
* Van Dyke advocates reducing regulatory burdens and streamlining permitting to address housing shortages, emphasizing the importance of balancing high-density developments with single-family homes.
* He critiques the Council’s handling of homelessness, calling for thoughtful, permanent solutions, and emphasizes supporting law enforcement through competitive pay, better funding, and community policing initiatives.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[Gaines: You paid about $24.5 million in tax dollars to Denver to address the city’s homelessness](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [COLORADO ACCOUNTABILITY PROJECT](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* In 2024, the State of Colorado disbursed over $24.5 million to Denver to address homelessness, highlighting significant state investment in the city's housing crisis.
* Efforts to obtain detailed accounts of Denver's homelessness spending reveal challenges in transparency, with local officials providing limited or inconsistent responses. The commentary questions the effectiveness of state and local expenditures, urging greater accountability and better tracking of funds used to tackle homelessness.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[Walcher: The unproud Western legacy of Jimmy Carter](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [GREGWALCHER.COM](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Jimmy Carter's Alaska National Interest Lands Conservation Act of 1980 set aside 157 million acres for federal protection, restricting energy development and public use, a decision Carter considered his most significant domestic achievement.
* Shortly after taking office, Carter targeted 19 Western water projects for de-authorization, including some already under construction, alienating rural and bipartisan Western constituencies and disrupting long-standing agreements.
* Carter’s policies, seen as attacks on the West and rural America, contributed to his 1980 election loss and the defeat of several Western Democratic senators and governors, solidifying the West as a Republican stronghold.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[McMahon: Mayor Johnston, the ‘reality’ of downtown Denver is it is not safe](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
_I don’t typically speak out about issues like this, because I’ve always believed that complaints without solutions don’t accomplish much._
_But after watching Mayor Mike Johnston’s press conference on Monday, I felt compelled to respond._
_His comment about the recent stabbings on the 16th Street Mall struck a nerve. He said, “I think there is a question of the difference between perception and reality, and that is part of what we have to address. I have folks who say, ‘I don’t like to come downtown. I don’t feel safe.’ And I say, ‘Really, when did you come downtown last?’ And they say 2021. I say, ‘Okay, well, it is a very different downtown than what you saw three years ago.’”_ __**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**ONLY ON ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE ⤵️**
**[New wolf pack arrival in Western Colorado is just in time for calving season](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****BY LINDY BROWNING | STATE | JANUARY 13** _On Saturday, Jan. 11, Colorado Parks and Wildlife put out a press release that they were going to begin trapping and transporting operations to bring up to 15 Canadian Grey Wolves from British Columbia, Canada, to Western Colorado._
**[Lundberg: Should we expect more of the same in 75th legislative session?](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****BY KEVIN LUNDBERG****| COMMENTARY | JANUARY 13** _As Colorado’s 2025 legislative session began, the governor and leaders in both houses laid out their agendas. The legislators’ remarks were somewhat predictable, talking about unity and cooperation — a good place to start, but more lip service than reality._ __
**[It’s a sad day in Colorado’: Sen. Pelton recalls Bill McCartney era at Colorado](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****BY BRIAN PORTER****| LOCAL | JANUARY 13** _It wasn’t an easy life growing up on the Eastern Plains of Colorado, Sen. Byron Pelton recalled Monday for the Colorado Senate._ _He became a fan of the Colorado Buffaloes and almost no one then was on the Eastern Plains, and still isn’t today._ __

**Feel like you have a unique story or commentary to share?** We'd love to talk with you about becoming an RMV Contributor. Reply to this email (or reach out to [brian@rockymountainvoice.com](mailto:brian@rockymountainvoice.com?subject=Contributor%20Interest%20\(via%20Mountain%20Minute\))) or send us a message on social media to chat!
|
---
|
---
**🇺🇸 DID YOU KNOW?** For Ulysses S. Grant’s second inauguration in 1873, organizers placed **hundreds of caged canaries** along Pennsylvania Avenue as part of the festivities. Tragically, the bitter cold killed many of the birds before the event concluded.
|
---
**CARTOON OF THE DAY**
|
---
**QUOTE OF THE DAY** “We must not be enemies. Though passion may have strained it must not break our bonds of affection. The mystic chords of memory, stretching from every battlefield and patriot grave to every living heart and hearthstone all over this broad land, will yet swell the chorus of the Union, when again touched, as surely they will be, by the better angels of our nature." — President Abraham Lincoln, first inauguration in March, 1861
|
---

name: Tim Scott
subject: Whatever makes the most sense for you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRSC
body:
your early investment in victory

Friend, I officially became NRSC Chairman a couple of weeks ago.

**_[The goal for my first month is simple: Make this our best January ever.](https://email.alerts.therepublicanteam.com/c/eJyMkUGP2yAQhX8NvhExgO3k4MNKVm5bVdVKPaIJkHgqgy0Y7_79iqR77w3BzHvve_x-d-9IK-XHD0xx4sKuVJdL9S5tmReJVfoFqTitdC8VSOid6cJkRx3M0MUJRjNcemUH1S3T7XIfvYEbnL3xCHcbAIKFiPcwet3rjqamowB60Pai1MnjbTgDGrj4EeIZhFW4xsL1xEsscT9uK3nMHDGd_Ja6dVqY9yrMm9BXoa81-qPE0xflEkObEPra4gt9_YNZ9zJRkC8UrjLKHdueuR6cXIqBjiTMHBPSKvTQLut2FB-FmTedXkW0leeTx7QjPbIw8z8K6zK64mWbSxSePtCMPsH9fHNc_cbsXnmeEuyFmT9-fWj53cO39pY5ZhZmvh85FKRK-SH0UBpfaWczczmi0AMm3rdKTFtLYrsyhf2L1pXqMAqrHg3m2RX_939-TvpvAAAA___0IqsT)_**

With this Senate map, we simply can't afford to get outraised 10:1, 5:1, or even 2:1 by the Democrats. It happened last year. It can't happen this time.

I know we can flip the script, friend, but there's a hitch: Halfway through the month, we're around $52,000 short of where we need to be.

body:

name: Rudy Giuliani
subject: President Trump: “Save Rudy!”
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rudy Giuliani Freedom Fund Legal Defense Trust
body:
The trial of my life begins this week. And I don’t get a jury!
| [](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=104b6c469d67443cb0537121206e77b5&_e=4dPEF09QsOS9JujoWiEVIFg3llw-ZWdGYwrzCf5YxYN8n5D-CNA7D3HxC35OwpdxPluR375MhWU_00Q2dlY3GRAarJGylKmCVTshiuDdFfcPv3yvSaxYVxxSg5xQDwxc0nX7Lzrl2M1o0dxiLrkfWp5S9mKsfOT-W8VB9L6qzPZ6_e5tmKW0Z6RYYFwouuCT273FUqhY1rmuV2z6jQyiX_SrzInv1ZQHtkatY-sK_vT-B1P31B5lOKXEzPKYtEHx-WgVEiuKyLrsKKkOT2UK0cXJljTP4xFb4a95-_qEWnOUNqg6IMR1P_SwamiJtBllBYZkaBNFOAcff-P5w5lcs5o50cu6zYCdEZXb3TfTl0QHIHQzmqXuooRPH3QKSlgn)
---
Fellow Patriot,
---
With the trial of my life set to begin, President Donald J. Trump just said two powerful words for the entire nation to hear...
---
**_“SAVE RUDY!”_**
---
On Thursday, I will be on trial – __without_ a jury, mind you_ – to determine whether or not I still have a home to live in. It’s a trial to save my home and a trial to fight for my basic freedoms and rights.
---
_But more than that, our nation’s very system of justice will also be on trial._
---
If the Radical Left Democrats can get away with destroying me in a weaponized court without a jury – and possibly imprisoning me – solely because I served as President Trump’s lawyer, then the rule of law is DEAD in America.
---
**Just last week, a bloodthirsty left-wing judge threatened me with contempt and imprisonment if I didn’t recite her dictated false version of covered-up massive evidence of voter fraud.**
---
I had a choice to make: _I could recite the state-sanctioned propaganda like an obedient little dog and be rewarded with a pat on the head. Or, I could have the courage to stand for what I know to be true._
---
When I remind you of this Bible verse, you’ll know exactly which choice I made: "_For what shall it profit a man, if he shall gain the whole world, and lose his own soul?_ " (Mark 8:36)
---
You see, I refuse to give up on America. That’s why I’m going on trial to fight to save my home, my liberty, and __our__ Republic.
---
_Are YOU with me?_ Will you join the cause to “SAVE RUDY?”
---
**[Please make a contribution to my Legal Defense Trust – the Rudy Giuliani Freedom Fund](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=104b6c469d67443cb0537121206e77b5&_e=4dPEF09QsOS9JujoWiEVIFg3llw-ZWdGYwrzCf5YxYN8n5D-CNA7D3HxC35OwpdxPluR375MhWU_00Q2dlY3GRAarJGylKmCVTshiuDdFfcPv3yvSaxYVxxSg5xQDwxc0nX7Lzrl2M1o0dxiLrkfWp5S9mKsfOT-W8VB9L6qzPZ6_e5tmKW0Z6RYYFwouuCT273FUqhY1rmuV2z6jQyiX0wj2w_dWZUftK3V5DpvL2fyU7t1ksVTxkfDs7M07M38x_QlWO-VhYAL8gO30e5Nf5Pn5vWFDNQ3PswVfcWL4OuYAiEXkm6Kg_Va3NM74-jHMgHjk78FTr91KvrcqRtMgCuAan1R--ohdeJHpNhyi57Nbapb9gjAJRI0uafuYgQ7) – as I prepare for the trial that will determine whether I still even have a home to live in. **

clean_body: The trial of my life begins this week. And I don’t get a jury! | Fellow Patriot, With the trial of my life set to begin, President Donald J. Trump just said two powerful words for the entire nation to hear... “SAVE RUDY!” On Thursday, I will be on trial – without a jury, mind you – to determine whether or not I still have a home to live in. It’s a trial to save my home and a trial to fight for my basic freedoms and rights. But more than that, our nation’s very system of justice will also be on trial. If the Radical Left Democrats can get away with destroying me in a weaponized court without a jury – and possibly imprisoning me – solely because I served as President Trump’s lawyer, then the rule of law is DEAD in America. Just last week, a bloodthirsty left-wing judge threatened me with contempt and imprisonment if I didn’t recite her dictated false version of covered-up massive evidence of voter fraud. I had a choice to make: I could recite the state-sanctioned propaganda like an obedient little dog and be rewarded with a pat on the head. Or, I could have the courage to stand for what I know to be true. When I remind you of this Bible verse, you’ll know exactly which choice I made: "For what shall it profit a man, if he shall gain the whole world, and lose his own soul? " (Mark 8:36) You see, I refuse to give up on America. That’s why I’m going on trial to fight to save my home, my liberty, and our Republic. Are YOU with me? Will you join the cause to “SAVE RUDY?” – as I prepare for the trial that will determine whether I still even have a home to live in. Thank you and God bless you, Rudy Giuliani Rudy Giuliani Freedom Fund Paid for by Rudy Giuliani Freedom Fund Legal Defense Trust

name: Ben Samuels
subject: High-tax states are costing Democrats
body:
The Republican push for less government spending is vapid. But by talking about government inefficiency, they're touching on issues that clearly resonate with voters.

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZW5zYW11ZWxzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.h_YNooQydDe2xcOoBTP8lbGmBER_NYa94mejpRTJ-oo?) for more
---
# [High-tax states are costing Democrats](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=154490083&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU0NDkwMDgzLCJpYXQiOjE3MzY5NDY3ODMsImV4cCI6MTczOTUzODc4MywiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.7qqME8jAt5-4I0LaDFQ1F9BTw8WyUAcbHw11gMioZ-Q)
### The Republican push for less government spending is vapid. But by talking about government inefficiency, they're touching on issues that clearly resonate with voters.
| | [Ben Samuels](https://substack.com/@bensamuels)
---
| Jan 15
---
| [](https://substack.com/@bensamuels)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvYmVuc2FtdWVscy9wL2hpZ2gtdGF4LXN0YXRlcy1hcmUtY29zdGluZy1kZW1vY3JhdHM_.4Tllvw_dpvQEYO1VjcKDi1CigGcUPHJ1I4rF7Gz_hEo?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/bensamuels/p/high-tax-states-are-costing-democrats?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

A lot has been written about [why](https://substack.com/redirect/61b3661a-52d0-459a-b19d-02d0a423e971?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [Kamala](https://substack.com/redirect/46878280-3954-4c47-aab6-aec9b10f391e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [Harris](https://substack.com/redirect/ca2853ba-072c-451d-96b1-6e2d80acef9d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [lost](https://substack.com/redirect/d4866354-9ac2-4821-95cb-3eab1185f367?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [the](https://substack.com/redirect/23d80564-7270-492b-b95c-980948459605?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [2024](https://substack.com/redirect/b7e74560-9060-4335-b023-ef9cd7ebdfe1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [Presidential](https://substack.com/redirect/b4888ee0-d25a-4cbd-9168-95e728bb9839?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [election](https://substack.com/redirect/0b133d38-025d-41fc-bdbd-72ef7f776b48?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). The reality, of course, is that there are _lots_ of reasons that she didn't win.1
But all of these articles are missing an important point. **The[largest states](https://substack.com/redirect/c6b0d5ee-1a7f-49be-b4f3-102abba1900c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that Democrats consistently control--California,**2**New York, Illinois, and New Jersey --have some of the highest tax burdens in the country. **
For all the money its residents pay in taxes, **it 's not clear that the quality of life is any better than in places like Texas and Florida**, where the tax burden is substantially lower. In many ways, it might be worse.
**This is something that Democrats need to seriously grapple with** , and I don't see enough Democrats doing that yet.
[Subscribe now](https://substack.com/redirect/735a6b40-474a-463a-88b7-454d57612429?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
### Comparing California and New York to Texas and Florida
About [115 million people](https://substack.com/redirect/c6b0d5ee-1a7f-49be-b4f3-102abba1900c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--more than a third of the country--live in California, Texas, Florida, and New York. But the tax profile of California and New York vs. Texas and Florida could hardly be more different.
In California, top earners pay [13.3% income tax rates](https://substack.com/redirect/d9e6e70a-e6c9-49b9-8b17-f6e58dc33d7c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), on top of federal taxes. In New York, [top earners pay 10.9%](https://substack.com/redirect/5f89f851-0925-4f0d-95c7-34bf3bbebca4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and that rate is higher if you live in New York City, where top earners can pay as much as [52% in income tax](https://substack.com/redirect/8aefda39-e0b9-470a-81ad-6c85d6a2457d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) across federal, state, and local taxes.
In Texas and Florida, the income tax rates are 0% and 0%, respectively.
Yes, in California and New York, only the highest earners pay that much in income taxes. But even for the median taxpayer, across all taxes--income tax, property tax, sales tax, etc.--the tax burden in California and New York is high, and the tax burden in Texas and Florida is low.3
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/3c329452-f34a-49c1-949d-d4c05abc5040?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
### What do California, New York, Illinois, and New Jersey have to show for their tax rates?
Here's a side-by-side comparison of metrics in the largest Democratic states vs. the largest Republican ones.4 (I'm including Missouri too, just for comparison and context, since many of you also live here.)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/859e37a3-20bd-4428-b76b-9be78690e432?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
It's not that things are uniformly worse in places like California, New York, Illinois, and New Jersey relative to Texas and Florida. There are jarring differences, for instance, in health insurance coverage5 and life expectancy.
**The challenge is that for all the taxes Californians and New Yorkers pay, it 's not clear that things are any better. At best, it's kind of a mixed bag.** And that should have people in California and New York--and across the country--asking questions.
A few things to point out from this table:
* This is, of course, reductive. There is a lot more than just these metrics in evaluating which states are better places to live.
* There are certain comparisons, namely education, that I would've liked to make but where there isn't a good side-by-side way of doing it.6

### Press coverage of blue-state governance
If you're trying to defend blue-state governance, there are a lot of headlines that don't help. Take, for instance, train infrastructure.
In New York City, extensions to the Second Avenue Subway have been [over budget and decades behind schedule](https://substack.com/redirect/c4f63ba4-00be-406b-a304-ad149c0928d2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and train spending there is [shockingly inefficient relative to similar infrastructure projects in other countries](https://substack.com/redirect/3a69ac1a-e596-4946-be3a-1ee51a05efc2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). In California, the [high-speed rail project](https://substack.com/redirect/79bfc4ba-5bfe-4ead-88ee-147a45312706?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) has been a cosmic flop. Headlines like these don't exactly illustrate disciplined taxpayer spending:
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/bf0315f9-4ad9-42ab-9e3d-39352501504b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
It's not like the privately owned [Florida high-speed rail project](https://substack.com/redirect/5275b12b-ca7c-4745-9fcd-426da13585ac?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) was a paragon of financial restraint: the cost was about [$3.5 billion (3.3×) over the initial projections](https://substack.com/redirect/f1b7b4eb-f354-415b-8430-75ebe60e79bb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). But: the project was completed and has been operating for a few years--even if [it hasn't been entirely successful](https://substack.com/redirect/6d2bfe4b-889a-40c2-998a-ec0d71de876c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).7
Likewise, in an election where [immigration was a key issue](https://substack.com/redirect/f7233c42-16b0-42a8-b4e8-13cd83009e43?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), headlines like these don't inspire much confidence either--especially when the Governors of Florida and Texas [were as aggressive on immigration as they were](https://substack.com/redirect/3b1fcb1d-9759-422c-8b02-fb9c78a6e11d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg):
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/b1030ae8-8b59-4ba8-a8bb-37ea8e98eef7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Americans very consistently believe that [they pay too much in taxes](https://substack.com/redirect/c6669169-914f-4730-870d-ad0d50ee6d96?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). [58% of Americans believe that their tax dollars are spent inefficiently](https://substack.com/redirect/6030df1c-695e-4dce-b164-ca420970fe63?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg); only 18% believe they're spent efficiently.8
And when you have pretty prominent examples like these, [and](https://substack.com/redirect/1cd8fbbd-75f7-420e-be87-ed946559bd80?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [a](https://substack.com/redirect/d3c2ddaa-4291-47a6-b749-5499bf91bad0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [bunch](https://substack.com/redirect/01fc0e57-4597-4760-8d77-09abd8fd81a4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [of](https://substack.com/redirect/f3cd7ab0-3540-43dc-8109-06678dc0bdd7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [others](https://substack.com/redirect/c6ccaf96-f9aa-453f-a7cf-5368288cdb2a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), that people can point to demonstrating resources being spent inefficiently? That shapes the narrative in powerful ways.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=154490083&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU0NDkwMDgzLCJpYXQiOjE3MzY5NDY3ODMsImV4cCI6MTczOTUzODc4MywiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.7qqME8jAt5-4I0LaDFQ1F9BTw8WyUAcbHw11gMioZ-Q)
### Wait, so are you a fan of [DOGE](https://substack.com/redirect/a65f2602-49d8-42ce-b382-3af299a7e80b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) now?
No. Not even a little.9
1. First of all, the nature of federal expenses is vastly different than what happens at the state level. The majority of federal expenses fund just three things: Social Security, Medicare, and military/defense. Cutting staff does nothing to save money on the first two, and the [military is dealing with a staffing ](https://substack.com/redirect/344b7c6b-c810-4d0e-9631-e02e84fa5ca5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[shortage](https://substack.com/redirect/344b7c6b-c810-4d0e-9631-e02e84fa5ca5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[ right now](https://substack.com/redirect/344b7c6b-c810-4d0e-9631-e02e84fa5ca5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).10
2. Second, and more to the point, I believe that government has a hugely important role to play in improving our lives.11

With that said, can the government be operating more efficiently than it is? Without question. One glaring example: the bipartisan infrastructure bill set aside $42 billion for broadband internet access, and that money has resulted in [zero people getting broadband internet access](https://substack.com/redirect/bb18cb08-1b54-49f6-b415-f15ecc12e999?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). (Nothing the DOGE people are talking about addresses issues like these.)
People want their tax dollars spent well. And the issue, really, is that **Democrats don 't have enough credibility on this right now**. Voters who look to places like California and New York see too many examples of states that aren't being run particularly efficiently.
### What's the solution here?
1. **Democrats need to focus on reducing costs!** That's not just government expenses--how do you run state governments more efficiently?--but also for individuals. Housing, for everyone, has become too expensive, and [it's by far the largest chunk of individual spending](https://substack.com/redirect/eaee28b5-438d-4720-938d-10c27b8518af?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).12 Reducing the burden of regulation and zoning to allow for more housing reduces costs of living.13
2. I've always respected Rep. [Jared Golden](https://substack.com/redirect/c639e176-4e74-4988-8538-e3b405f35378?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ([Maine-02](https://substack.com/redirect/957eb7ad-bd4b-4e0f-92f5-d4ed58b9a085?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)), and not just because no other Democrat represents a more Republican-leaning district. His [support for fiscal restraint](https://substack.com/redirect/679f5985-4dfc-41ff-82dd-eef3ccc12553?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) isn't inconsistent with support for programs like affordable housing and SNAP. Empirically, his brand of politics is what wins tough elections.
3. It's not that issues like climate change, abortion access, LGBTQ rights, etc. don't matter. They obviously do, a great deal. But even though those issues aren't the primary movers for voters right now, politically it's the main pitch I hear for why people should live in California and New York.

**Democrats have to demonstrate that they can improve quality of life. That means low costs for everyone and basic human rights for all.**
I worry a lot about the next four years. But in the immediate term, Democrats need to pivot and focus more on reducing costs, reducing taxes, and showing voters that they're able to deliver meaningful change for their constituents.
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZW5zYW11ZWxzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9hY2NvdW50IiwicCI6MTU0NDkwMDgzLCJzIjoyNDQwMzk4LCJmIjp0cnVlLCJ1Ijo5MDUwMTE5MiwiaWF0IjoxNzM2OTQ2NzgzLCJleHAiOjE3Mzk1Mzg3ODMsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9.jbBEt-prjWdNvU1fGJha_SyzbvTHO3Dkjm7Oe4BED18?)
_**Feel free to share this post with someone who will find this interesting.** (If you're reading this email because someone sent it to you, [please consider subscribing](https://substack.com/redirect/735a6b40-474a-463a-88b7-454d57612429?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).)_
_For press inquiries, please contact press@ben-samuels.com._
1
I've written earlier about [Democrats failing to reach young men](https://substack.com/redirect/a3a15026-360b-4416-9c92-d5ce0905d080?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). That proved to be, unfortunately, [prescient](https://substack.com/redirect/55037d0b-3230-4921-832d-7a396b993a6c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
2
I feel terrible for the people impacted by these fires in Los Angeles, and nothing I'm writing here should be taken otherwise. I've had a bunch of people I know lose their houses; I hope people are able to rebuild, though it's tough to know what the path forward is right now.
For people who don't have ties to California, I worry that there's fatigue around wildfires-on-the-West-Coast stories, since they seem to happen every year. But this is so much worse than anything we've seen before.
3
It's a relatively complicated calculation, and the assumptions that people make around consumption, etc. vary, so different sources have different answers here. WalletHub, for instance, [has a slightly different ranking](https://substack.com/redirect/77834ffc-6af1-45f2-8563-034ab9c051ae?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
I'm also keenly aware that a lot of the organizations that analyze tax policy do so with a political agenda in mind. But no matter how you slice it: the tax burden in California and New York is higher than the tax burden in Texas and Florida.
4
First, here are the sources for everything here: [median home price](https://substack.com/redirect/259911f4-6c86-4bb3-a56c-61c21e63df5a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [unemployment rate](https://substack.com/redirect/eecd992b-2e87-4c3e-ab96-b7ef31b8d282?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [average commute time](https://substack.com/redirect/17b58413-60f4-4f22-ae21-410c42f5b2b7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [robberies per 100,000](https://substack.com/redirect/59d361db-93cc-4284-a67d-508b04c716e0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [life expectancy](https://substack.com/redirect/09a0203f-a479-4dcc-b02c-90513db14f3d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and the [percentage of residents living without health insurance](https://substack.com/redirect/416fcb32-d01c-4b05-af0c-82fcbce42114?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
The differences in commute times may not seem like much, but over the course of a year, they add up. People in New York commute 5.6 minutes more than people in Texas each way. Over the course of a year, that's about 47 hours of additional commuting--more than a full work week. (The difference between New York and Missouri amounts to 77.5 hours per year.)
5
It's especially jarring in Florida, where the population is older, meaning that a much higher percentage is on Medicare. What that means is that a huge chunk of the state's younger population are uninsured.
6
Polling in this context has its issues, since it's not necessarily correlated with outcomes. But in a recent California poll, [47% say the state's K-12 education is getting worse](https://substack.com/redirect/d756dacd-600d-4d7f-9df2-5ad9bb45c229?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (vs. 13% who say it's getting better). In Texas, [84% of parents of K-12 students are very or somewhat satisfied](https://substack.com/redirect/815b461d-9494-42fb-9cac-89cfb38997b1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) with the quality of their child's education.
This is an apples-to-oranges comparison, so it's hard to learn much from this, but it does seem clear that higher taxes aren't _necessarily_ leading to more parents satisfied with the state of public education.
7
It [hasn't been entirely successful](https://substack.com/redirect/c510d9a3-61cc-4f94-a62c-d4a2535cbc24?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), at least not yet, but for the future of U.S. infrastructure investment, I very much hope it will be. I think and hope we can chart a path forward for more private-sector investments in mass transit.
8
There's a lot to unpack here when you dig into this data. 65% of federal spending goes to four things: [Social Security, defense, Medicare, and paying interest on debt](https://substack.com/redirect/6cb303cc-a1bc-409b-a21d-7e36fd2b9755?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). In order to cut federal spending in any material way, therefore, the federal government would have to cut spending in (at least) one of those areas.
But the details of this polling demonstrate that although people don't think federal spending is efficient, [Americans also believe that too ](https://substack.com/redirect/03ec23dd-a4dc-458a-b29d-247c97c53720?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[little](https://substack.com/redirect/03ec23dd-a4dc-458a-b29d-247c97c53720?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[ is spent across the board](https://substack.com/redirect/03ec23dd-a4dc-458a-b29d-247c97c53720?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg):
* 58% say too little is spent on Social Security (vs. 7% who say too much)
* 58% say too little is spent on health care (vs. 9% who say too much)
* 30% say too little is spent on military and national defense (vs. 26% who say too much)
* 41% say too little is spent on economic and income assistance like [SNAP](https://substack.com/redirect/884cab1c-0d06-446c-897b-2e4c3d4dfe7e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (vs. 22% who say too much)
* 42% say too little is spent on infrastructure (vs. 12% who say too much)
* 56% say too little is spent on education (vs. 10% who say too much)

There are a bunch of reasons for this, not least of which is that when options are laid out one-by-one, there's no sense of tradeoffs being made. In a vacuum, sure, who doesn't want more education spending and more defense spending and more Social Security and more spending on National Parks!
9
DOGE, for those of you who have the fortune of not knowing, stands for the [Department of Government Efficiency](https://substack.com/redirect/a65f2602-49d8-42ce-b382-3af299a7e80b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). It's run by the sort of people who aren't nearly as competent as they think they are, and unironically refer to themselves as geniuses.
10
Indiscriminately taking a weedwacker to federal expenses is dangerous and would be exceedingly unpopular.
Not for nothing: there are plenty of places where _more_ federal employees would be helpful. I submitted a records request that took over a year to work through the [National Archives and Records Administration](https://substack.com/redirect/6a7bf451-14c3-4d2a-a048-750f924dabd3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), the [National Personnel Records Center](https://substack.com/redirect/96ce096a-a255-428f-bf78-6981d5f1c5ae?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and the [Department of the Navy](https://substack.com/redirect/00c8cb06-1e2b-4e3e-9d51-262c4295e2d4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). A lot of that stemmed from staffing shortages, especially coming out of the pandemic, that made it difficult for federal employees to get to my request.
Can this be run more efficiently? Absolutely. But just wholesale firing government employees would make things much worse, not better.
11
I think a lot of the "libertarians" who espouse small-government views are basically just libertarians of convenience, and [they see the value of the government when they worry that government inaction will cost them](https://substack.com/redirect/78789cc8-baf9-4d4b-83f2-8e0e1e924b0a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
12
[50% of all individual spending is just two things](https://substack.com/redirect/eaee28b5-438d-4720-938d-10c27b8518af?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg): housing and transportation. If you include food, those three categories account for about 63% of total spending.
13
Of the [10 most expensive states for housing](https://substack.com/redirect/259911f4-6c86-4bb3-a56c-61c21e63df5a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), seven are reliably Democratic, one is Republican (Utah), and two are purple-ish (New Hampshire and Colorado). Washington, D.C. would also be on the list if it were a state.
Some of this is simply a function of urban areas being more expensive and more Democratic, for reasons that have little to do with cost of living. But nonetheless, there's much, much more that has to be done on housing affordability, everywhere across the country.

---

Housing, for everyone, has become too expensive, and Reducing the burden of regulation and zoning to allow for more housing reduces costs of living.13 2. I've always respected Rep. (), and not just because no other Democrat represents a more Republican-leaning district. His isn't inconsistent with support for programs like affordable housing and SNAP. Empirically, his brand of politics is what wins tough elections. 3. It's not that issues like climate change, abortion access, LGBTQ rights, etc. don't matter. They obviously do, a great deal. But even though those issues aren't the primary movers for voters right now, politically it's the main pitch I hear for why people should live in California and New York. Democrats have to demonstrate that they can improve quality of life. That means low costs for everyone and basic human rights for all. I worry a lot about the next four years. But in the immediate term, Democrats need to pivot and focus more on reducing costs, reducing taxes, and showing voters that they're able to deliver meaningful change for their constituents. ? Feel free to share this post with someone who will find this interesting. (If you're reading this email because someone sent it to you, .) For press inquiries, please contact press@ben-samuels.com. 1 I've written earlier about . That proved to be, unfortunately, . 2 I feel terrible for the people impacted by these fires in Los Angeles, and nothing I'm writing here should be taken otherwise. I've had a bunch of people I know lose their houses; I hope people are able to rebuild, though it's tough to know what the path forward is right now. For people who don't have ties to California, I worry that there's fatigue around wildfires-on-the-West-Coast stories, since they seem to happen every year. But this is so much worse than anything we've seen before. 3 It's a relatively complicated calculation, and the assumptions that people make around consumption, etc. vary, so different sources have different answers here. WalletHub, for instance, . I'm also keenly aware that a lot of the organizations that analyze tax policy do so with a political agenda in mind. But no matter how you slice it: the tax burden in California and New York is higher than the tax burden in Texas and Florida. 4 First, here are the sources for everything here: , , , , , and the . The differences in commute times may not seem like much, but over the course of a year, they add up. People in New York commute 5.6 minutes more than people in Texas each way. Over the course of a year, that's about 47 hours of additional commuting--more than a full work week. (The difference between New York and Missouri amounts to 77.5 hours per year.) 5 It's especially jarring in Florida, where the population is older, meaning that a much higher percentage is on Medicare. What that means is that a huge chunk of the state's younger population are uninsured. 6 Polling in this context has its issues, since it's not necessarily correlated with outcomes. But in a recent California poll, (vs. 13% who say it's getting better). In Texas, with the quality of their child's education. This is an apples-to-oranges comparison, so it's hard to learn much from this, but it does seem clear that higher taxes aren't necessarily leading to more parents satisfied with the state of public education. 7 It , at least not yet, but for the future of U.S. infrastructure investment, I very much hope it will be. I think and hope we can chart a path forward for more private-sector investments in mass transit. 8 There's a lot to unpack here when you dig into this data. 65% of federal spending goes to four things: . In order to cut federal spending in any material way, therefore, the federal government would have to cut spending in (at least) one of those areas. But the details of this polling demonstrate that although people don't think federal spending is efficient, : 58% say too little is spent on Social Security (vs. 7% who say too much) 58% say too little is spent on health care (vs. 9% who say too much) 30% say too little is spent on military and national defense (vs. 26% who say too much) 41% say too little is spent on economic and income assistance like (vs. 22% who say too much) 42% say too little is spent on infrastructure (vs. 12% who say too much) 56% say too little is spent on education (vs. 10% who say too much) There are a bunch of reasons for this, not least of which is that when options are laid out one-by-one, there's no sense of tradeoffs being made. In a vacuum, sure, who doesn't want more education spending and more defense spending and more Social Security and more spending on National Parks! 9 DOGE, for those of you who have the fortune of not knowing, stands for the . It's run by the sort of people who aren't nearly as competent as they think they are, and unironically refer to themselves as geniuses. 10 Indiscriminately taking a weedwacker to federal expenses is dangerous and would be exceedingly unpopular. Not for nothing: there are plenty of places where more federal employees would be helpful. I submitted a records request that took over a year to work through the , the , and the . A lot of that stemmed from staffing shortages, especially coming out of the pandemic, that made it difficult for federal employees to get to my request. Can this be run more efficiently? Absolutely. But just wholesale firing government employees would make things much worse, not better. 11 I think a lot of the "libertarians" who espouse small-government views are basically just libertarians of convenience, and . 12 : housing and transportation. If you include food, those three categories account for about 63% of total spending. 13 Of the , seven are reliably Democratic, one is Republican (Utah), and two are purple-ish (New Hampshire and Colorado). Washington, D.C. would also be on the list if it were a state. Some of this is simply a function of urban areas being more expensive and more Democratic, for reasons that have little to do with cost of living. But nonetheless, there's much, much more that has to be done on housing affordability, everywhere across the country. | | | (C) 2025 Ben Samuels 548 Market Street PMB 72296, San Francisco, CA 94104 ? ?

| Candidate Training Dates: Take the First Step Toward Shaping Massachusetts' Future
---
---
| Massachusetts is at a turning point. A significant political realignment is underway, and our state is moving decisively to the right. Now is the time to act. Starting in February, the Massachusetts Republican Party will hold twice-monthly training sessions for those ready to run for municipal office in 2025 and beyond.
Municipal elections are the foundation of a successful political career, providing a pathway to higher office. Whether you're considering a run for school board, select board, city council, mayor--or even dog catcher--we want to help you succeed.
Our comprehensive candidate training sessions will cover essential topics such as political strategy, data, finance, and communications. You'll gain the tools and insights needed to run and win as a Republican in Massachusetts. Thanks to our generous donors these training sessions will be FREE to partake in.
Here are the details for our first four sessions:
Fall River 🗓️ February 1, 2025 ⏰ 12:00 PM 📍 VFW 486 Bedford Street, Fall River, MA
Holyoke 🗓️ February 15, 2025 ⏰ 10:00 AM 📍 Holyoke Public Library, 250 Chestnut Street, Holyoke, MA
Lawrence 🗓️ March 1, 2025 ⏰ 10:00 AM 📍 Parkside Apartments Community Room 260 Haverhill St, Lawrence, MA 01840
Worcester 🗓️ March 15, 2025 ⏰ 10:00 AM 📍 765 W Boylston Street, Floor 2, Worcester, MA
Don't miss this opportunity to shape the future of Massachusetts!
[Register Now](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001uj0h9f_ubjh8X59ReCsXNC0CFZ58iCL2I00FIssKNtqbDTEJB-P8sVcgu3tFxAG2RMzSH05Slb7SyKOiPCEqISfILkFPeH7GQWEqTBu-J-Mihnk8hcdzIwg0-ACrf55ddBf0FYtWKohMTQ-SXLUQAEliJiN0WFrcGVYnyNcSpmtfOSdBL5_vSSiQVRVcqRzOooK4YghoL5KVfK3c2Vijm3bogkHUQIcXVHAMgtvqsWwXkrf2B8VjamKbhAF9Xx-zgE5SkFsnCuIXc0sBwraR6zbFeRw8Mq8J&c=Yu0mlwZoQqKcP3gmgkA9SOB2LZW1Ow1xjK9CRTSfq48LjgrQrc_rpA==&ch=ZOBhSkAmt6lhiICSSt4d1BCF86FaYnjGa1W9bvnercGPh8ZfzHBVRg==)

---

CNN Republican Scott Jennings **[FIRED BACK at his democrat colleagues](https://substack.com/redirect/d15e72bd-5e8f-49af-9bac-c30f43217fbc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** who want California to receive wildfire aid from the Trump administration unconditionally.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/d15e72bd-5e8f-49af-9bac-c30f43217fbc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Host Abby Phillip pressed Jennings on his previous support for conditional aid to the state of California.
**["There ought to be conditions!"](https://substack.com/redirect/d15e72bd-5e8f-49af-9bac-c30f43217fbc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** Jennings said. "I want it to be conditional on knowing whether the state of California in this case is going to enact local policy decisions on forest management, on water resource management,..."
"I am not for a blank check to a state that has clearly failed and does not know how to manage fires!" Jennings added.
[Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/d15e72bd-5e8f-49af-9bac-c30f43217fbc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

# Could Musk Be Eyeing Silver?
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/cbd2a291-3270-4405-a833-fce9dc0dffd1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Elon Musk knows **[how to spot opportunities.](https://substack.com/redirect/cbd2a291-3270-4405-a833-fce9dc0dffd1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** He sees the trends. The patterns. The moments others miss. And now, some experts suggest silver might be in his sights.
Here's what's happening: Silver demand is surging, driven by tech and clean energy innovation. Supply chains are tightening, creating scarcity that could reshape markets.
Some believe **silver could become critical to industries Musk already leads.** But if silver is the next big move, what does that mean for your savings
There's a story unfolding here. One you don't want to miss. So if you're interested in that? **[Get your free guide here.](https://substack.com/redirect/cbd2a291-3270-4405-a833-fce9dc0dffd1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**

# **PISSED-OFF Katie Hopkins gives climate protester a brutal reality check...**
British conservative Katie Hopkins **[dropped a brutal truthbomb on a young climate protester](https://substack.com/redirect/79263e98-8bf8-40a5-a88b-5c7163f9374c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** during a tense discussion on the Patrick Bet-David Podcast.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/79263e98-8bf8-40a5-a88b-5c7163f9374c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
"Have you paid a single bill in your life?" Hopkins asked the young idealistic student, who is involved in extreme climate protests in the U.K. which include blocking roadways and throwing soup on priceless artwork in museums in the name of protesting fossil fuels.
"No..." the student answered.
Hopkins continued to tell her that she knew someone who was being rushed to the hospital for a medical emergency and the ambulance could not pass through a mob of climate protesters, **[resulting in her friend's death. ](https://substack.com/redirect/79263e98-8bf8-40a5-a88b-5c7163f9374c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
"She died because an ambulance couldn't get through because your protesters 'Just Stop Oil' were blocking the road," Hopkins explained. "Earlier you said that you were peaceful protesters,... My friend is dead, how do you answer that?"
**[Watch their unbelievable response:](https://substack.com/redirect/79263e98-8bf8-40a5-a88b-5c7163f9374c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[ Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/79263e98-8bf8-40a5-a88b-5c7163f9374c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

# Thanking my longtime friend, President Donald J. Trump
**By Rudy W. Giuliani**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/8c142abc-292f-406d-81b6-7a6b110ee09c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Trump posted a message of support for Giuliani, Sunday on Truth Social.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..KXs8SC5wg783BKjKU5t38iqi36XFURvAbuhg__BFtog?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=154883245)
In these tumultuous times, when so many in the political press and within the Democrat Party seem to be teaming up against me, it's comforting to know that I have friends who stand by me, unconditionally. I want to take this moment to express my deepest gratitude to Donald J. Trump, a man I've known for decades, and who has recently shown his unwavering support by posting "SAVE RUDY" on Truth Social.
Firstly, let me say, Mr. President, your gesture means more to me than you could possibly know. It's not just about the post; it's about the message it sends. It's about loyalty, something that's been the bedrock of our relationship from the very start. When I was the Mayor of New York City, you were there whenever we needed you. You understand the city's pulse, just as I did. We've walked through the streets of Manhattan together, not just as two New Yorkers, but as friends who shared a vision for a better, safer city...

name: Josh
subject: Building the bus while driving it
body:
What your average campaign has two years to do, we are doing in less than three months.
| | | | | [](https://wb7c9thbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001vi3AINISHMbFjf3cYzcOiE0KHBd7rM_tNQkvMSS_mLwP3SZHzrib_AoLSPy9srCQg-BmwIe27KEYFfWFZOVDbX4PuOn0OOSGnPSt4aCSQDFYK9iheQJGbtNMKPmMMouc9mxf6qPKmH9gcVyy6eCMNFLqDm2O8c9Difj4JAbfmqXT8zawOvK_wVQ1Fh4LvEidGptHmTKrycO8UzaBoB43KmmgxwpXqC85b4s9Imwz3Sk=&c=sWnKDhFZXzmpULxtIqzkR-I8ai1Eha4r-hNmIn4Jgdj1tjWFxtc8RQ==&ch=iZVZG7wQncjM2OD28m2N1FurWVUim4frcbIutMMpyhK0-XtEAK-PPQ==)
---
| | | | Team,
My name is Josh Weil, and I am the Dad, public school teacher, and Democrat running in the Special Election for Florida's 6th Congressional District - the bellwether first election of 2025 and Dems' first chance to fight back against MAGA's Project 2025.
By now, you are probably used to political emails that peddle a false sense of urgency for the most miniscule of deadlines, i.e.:
"if we don't hit our Monday morning 11 AM goal in 5 minutes \- this campaign AND our democracy is going to crash and burn 🔥😢"
When I say that meeting our Jan 15th mid-month goal today is beyond critical, know that this isn't one of those arbitrary rhetorical devices. As of today, our campaign has exactly 76 days until our April 1st election to raise enough money to have a viable shot at flipping this seat.
Florida Governor Ron DeSantis has intentionally given us as little time as possible to build our campaign. What your average campaign has two years to do, we are doing in less than three months.
But as a teacher who has had to develop online learning programs from scratch during COVID and effectively teach with the some of the smallest classroom budgets imaginable, I know what it's like to build the bus while driving it, and I know that this campaign will be ready to win on April 1st.
---
| | | | [Can I count on you to make a donation to help us "build the bus" and meet our mid-month goal so we can rapidly scale up in time for our election day in two months?](https://wb7c9thbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001vi3AINISHMbFjf3cYzcOiE0KHBd7rM_tNQkvMSS_mLwP3SZHzrib_AoLSPy9srCQfWDYOURd7rkblgUkNS-Scc40329m2TrzziSIOT61U1mxiiOSseZATAot3FtMDQGRTBxSpCIGJQDaxOspqExQIv33YUInooikrBIc05Ww-OhHWKOw1gwNgRJWOznwWu28AbpbMGQz9g_5OpD6tvS7eW7HGwhyO5aZcyzzEO7bz_zrNSG7aqQtnA==&c=sWnKDhFZXzmpULxtIqzkR-I8ai1Eha4r-hNmIn4Jgdj1tjWFxtc8RQ==&ch=iZVZG7wQncjM2OD28m2N1FurWVUim4frcbIutMMpyhK0-XtEAK-PPQ==)

clean_body: What your average campaign has two years to do, we are doing in less than three months. | | | | | == | | | | Team, My name is Josh Weil, and I am the Dad, public school teacher, and Democrat running in the Special Election for Florida's 6th Congressional District - the bellwether first election of 2025 and Dems' first chance to fight back against MAGA's Project 2025. By now, you are probably used to political emails that peddle a false sense of urgency for the most miniscule of deadlines, i.e.: "if we don't hit our Monday morning 11 AM goal in 5 minutes \- this campaign AND our democracy is going to crash and burn 🔥😢" When I say that meeting our Jan 15th mid-month goal today is beyond critical, know that this isn't one of those arbitrary rhetorical devices. As of today, our campaign has exactly 76 days until our April 1st election to raise enough money to have a viable shot at flipping this seat. Florida Governor Ron DeSantis has intentionally given us as little time as possible to build our campaign. What your average campaign has two years to do, we are doing in less than three months. But as a teacher who has had to develop online learning programs from scratch during COVID and effectively teach with the some of the smallest classroom budgets imaginable, I know what it's like to build the bus while driving it, and I know that this campaign will be ready to win on April 1st. | | | | == | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | | == | | == | | | | | == | | == | | | | | == | | == | | | | | == | | | | == | | | | Thank you in advance for your support for this ever effort to earn a sorely needed win for our movement and stop the Project 2025 agenda in its tracks. Josh Weil Democrat for Florida's 6th | | | | | == | | | | Josh Weil for Congress | 8381 Citrus Chase Drive | Orlando, FL 32836 US | |

---
---
| ICYMI: [Massachusetts Republican Chair Amy Carnevale was featured on Fox & Friends this week](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001sfe4y2Zs2NCRvPXFk-uCBYsQLds9xUM38_S-hyom9jul4lcoyE6bNlfHd-urJF99IIpNwJ2rPKXXQ4MJFKZgxpGuM99MXlWxEjHcoz5C-KLoX0QLlA1R3XMXsqWwqwhJzbUpuBHGzleN6h-nUe2YgZK0fj6_oasiM9G2p9Rm_wwZG_DCbdYRAg==&c=59YYbF_DH6YwU4AEMwm59ZT7dhEddm36jpUcZnU0MRalVI0-d28A3g==&ch=95P9F5EGKYvuhbfPNtMmTutU3FAK6ENaSHQcb0IZcMEOPeh6N9QtLg==), exposing the dangerous reality of Governor Healey's sanctuary state policies. During the interview, Amy discussed the shocking case of an illegal immigrant arrested for allegedly keeping over $1 million in drugs and guns in a Massachusetts shelter.
This is the direct result of the failed policies being pushed by Democrats. Our communities are at risk because of unchecked immigration and a lack of accountability in Governor Healey's administration. Amy Carnevale didn't hold back - she called out the failure of leadership that has put the safety of Massachusetts families on the line.
But we need your help to change this. We cannot allow this crisis to continue. Together, we can elect leaders who prioritize your safety, your community, and your values.
Here's how you can help:

1. Candidate Training - Beginning February 1st in Fall River, the MassGOP will host candidate training sessions around Massachusetts for individuals planning to run for office in 2025 or 2026. Explore the dates and locations and register for FREE here
2. Fund Republican Resistance - Your [contribution](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001sfe4y2Zs2NCRvPXFk-uCBYsQLds9xUM38_S-hyom9jul4lcoyE6bNuokNUm11v-0rlEHTJTn4ewpd-puPf_0EcMIcwTt9RwoVHKrH2J4Q-bvnmDomTGkWQm_Onu1qFY81t5_SFOD1s0LJbBl1tnNN-umNGcJTCioUmR6CnSUP-C45MO9Mps_qSm9zvzZAEUA&c=59YYbF_DH6YwU4AEMwm59ZT7dhEddm36jpUcZnU0MRalVI0-d28A3g==&ch=95P9F5EGKYvuhbfPNtMmTutU3FAK6ENaSHQcb0IZcMEOPeh6N9QtLg==) will directly fund efforts to push back against these dangerous policies and ensure we find & elect Republicans who will protect Massachusetts residents FIRST.
3. Share Amy's Message - Spread the word to friends and family about the importance of this fight. Together, we can make a real difference.

Let's take back control from the far-left policies that have put our state at risk. With your support, we can secure a safer future for Massachusetts. Thank you for standing with us.
