**A friend once told me, “People like us never think about running for office and that’s the problem.”**
That’s exactly why we created the Rooted in Community Campaign School.
We’ll train candidates on the fundamentals of running a grassroots campaign from communications to field work to fundraising and more―and we’ll ensure that each of these campaign strategies is rooted in community and reflects the needs of each candidate’s district, whether they’re running for local or statewide office.

clean_body: We’ll train candidates on the fundamentals of running a grassroots campaign. | | | Friend, Working people are fired up and ready to fight back in 2025. Next month, we’ll launch our Rooted in Community Campaign School―a free in-person program to train progressive candidates running for local or state office. Our Campaign School focuses on training women of color and BIPOC candidates with the lived experience needed to serve our communities. And while we had planned on training 15 candidates, we received a record number of applicants. We’re interviewing folks right now and want to expand February’s cohort to include 20 candidates. While the Campaign School is completely free for candidates, the program costs $400 per attendee. | If this past election taught us anything, it’s that we need more candidates who understand the needs of voters―and too often, everyday people don’t have the connections or resources to run for office and represent their communities. That’s why we end up with millionaires in elected office who can spend a lot of money on ads and mailers, but don’t actually understand the needs of the communities they serve. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately: | | | | | | | | | Let’s help shape the future of our communities together. -Rashida Tlaib Black, Indigenous, and other People of Color \---------- Forwarded message --------- From: Rashida Tlaib Date: Tue, Jan 14, 2025 Subject: We’ve budgeted to train 15 candidates, but we received a record number of applicants. Help us meet the moment. To: dpwillis67@gmail.com Friend, This week we’re interviewing applicants to our Rooted in Community Campaign School―a free in-person program to train progressive candidates running for local and state office. While we budgeted to train 15 candidates, we received a record number of applicants and we’d love to expand this year’s Campaign School further. The cost per trainee is just $400, which means that every donation, whether it’s $5 or $400, goes a long way toward training future leaders to run for office. As billionaires keep spending more to influence our elections, we need to keep supporting candidates who can’t typically afford the high costs of running for office. That’s why we provide our Rooted in Community Campaign School for free. The Rooted in Community Campaign School focuses on training women of color and BIPOC candidates with the lived experience needed to serve our communities. This month-long course will take place over five Saturdays in February and March. A friend once told me, “People like us never think about running for office and that’s the problem.” That’s exactly why we created the Rooted in Community Campaign School. We’ll train candidates on the fundamentals of running a grassroots campaign from communications to field work to fundraising and more―and we’ll ensure that each of these campaign strategies is rooted in community and reflects the needs of each candidate’s district, whether they’re running for local or statewide office. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately: | | | | | | | | | Join us and help shape the future of our communities together, Rashida Tlaib Black, Indigenous, and other People of Color | Rooted in Community Leadership PAC, PO Box 15845, Washington, DC 20003, not authorized by any candidate or candidates committee. | | Sent via . Marco Rubio has a long and dangerous track record of attacking reproductive rights. Now, President-elect Donald Trump has nominated him for Secretary of State—putting him in a position to shape global policies that impact reproductive health worldwide. [**We cannot allow him to use this role to further his anti-abortion agenda.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100438836/522764987/1734503619?sourceid=1150273&utm_campaign=25emege-0102aax-on&utm_medium=ea&utm_source=email&utm_content=nominations&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9DRlJSL0NGUlIvMS85NTk4MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMWU3YzQ3Zi02MGQzLWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-EZqW6PYn4SJ3xS1Dd4l7kOh3uUqJysTawHuXDwj2HM=&emci=f9ba704c-cad2-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&emdi=e1e7c47f-60d3-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&ceid=954237) | [**SAY NO TO RUBIO**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100438837/522764988/1734503619?sourceid=1150273&utm_campaign=25emege-0102aax-on&utm_medium=ea&utm_source=email&utm_content=nominations&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9DRlJSL0NGUlIvMS85NTk4MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMWU3YzQ3Zi02MGQzLWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-EZqW6PYn4SJ3xS1Dd4l7kOh3uUqJysTawHuXDwj2HM=&emci=f9ba704c-cad2-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&emdi=e1e7c47f-60d3-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&ceid=954237 "SAY NO TO RUBIO")
---
As Secretary of State, Rubio would oversee critical funding for international health programs and have direct influence over global reproductive rights policies. His record makes it clear: [**he will work to restrict reproductive freedom at every opportunity.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100438838/522764989/1734503619?sourceid=1150273&utm_campaign=25emege-0102aax-on&utm_medium=ea&utm_source=email&utm_content=nominations&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9DRlJSL0NGUlIvMS85NTk4MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMWU3YzQ3Zi02MGQzLWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-EZqW6PYn4SJ3xS1Dd4l7kOh3uUqJysTawHuXDwj2HM=&emci=f9ba704c-cad2-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&emdi=e1e7c47f-60d3-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&ceid=954237)

🚫 **Rubio's Record on Reproductive Rights:**
* Co-sponsored a **national abortion ban** in Congress.
* Sponsored nearly **two dozen anti-abortion bills in the last Congress alone,** including efforts to restrict access to medication abortion and impose unnecessary regulations on providers.
* Has openly stated he believes **life begins at conception** and that IVF poses a "very difficult bioethical issue."
* Joined multiple **legal efforts to overturn abortion rights,** including urging the U.S. Supreme Court to dismantle _Roe v. Wade._

[**CONTACT YOUR SENATORS**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100438839/522764990/1734503619?sourceid=1150273&utm_campaign=25emege-0102aax-on&utm_medium=ea&utm_source=email&utm_content=nominations&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9DRlJSL0NGUlIvMS85NTk4MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMWU3YzQ3Zi02MGQzLWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-EZqW6PYn4SJ3xS1Dd4l7kOh3uUqJysTawHuXDwj2HM=&emci=f9ba704c-cad2-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&emdi=e1e7c47f-60d3-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&ceid=954237 "CONTACT YOUR SENATORS")

name: MassGOP
subject: Healey-Driscoll Administration Takes Action on Reforms Long Advocated by Republicans After Two Years of Neglect
body:
| | | | |
---
| | [](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001lGuXSaw6LM1uhLZ3X08kywhqLgTWplEN8Zc2sbOM7wm-moNg9XwrghPK7oA5w_rhqZh_DnozyQGWQJbO4kYwRiL938NspT2qyPO9T1bXulYfcy5qtZKFFY24OEkiddie50lp8MScbaB1KQYupVKgGw==&c=dp8lUYWj1hKC3twfePElPEYUK1jARaYXGMS4iY0nIyF7Ie9Ov1SaYw==&ch=R1I_yomllvlZhQ5Aj-bmPEQt8vo52uSxCe1gjyCaVkIYbEjfoX1f3A==)
---
| Healey-Driscoll Administration Takes Action on Reforms Long Advocated by Republicans After Two Years of Neglect
---
---
|
### Boston, MA - Today, the Healey-Driscoll Administration announced reforms that Republicans have been tirelessly advocating for the past two years. These reforms come after the administration's reckless spending of $2.5 billion in taxpayer dollars, all while perpetuating a humanitarian crisis that has seen thousands of "serious incidents" including rape, drug trafficking, gun charges, and physical assaults.

### For two years, Republican legislators have warned of the dangers posed by the administration's lack of action, only to have those warnings ignored by the Democratic supermajority. Now, after the release of damaging information that has been known to the administration for some time, the Healey-Driscoll Administration is finally acting -- but only in half measures and after the damage has been done.

### MassGOP Chair Amy Carnevale commented on the proposed reforms, stating: "The reforms are a step in the right direction, but unfortunately leave a wide open loophole. In the Governor's reform, she is requesting a three month residency requirement to qualify for emergency housing. However, this can be replaced if the individual 'show[s] an intent to remain in Massachusetts,' which seemingly will allow anyone access to the right to shelter program so long as they say they plan to stay in Massachusetts after they leave the program. It seems like the residency requirements are more of a show than a meaningful policy change."
###

### "It's a shame that the Healey-Driscoll Administration took this long to come to terms with the gravity of the situation. And it's an even bigger shame that it was only after the information they've known for the past two years became public that they finally decided to take action. If Democrats had listened to the calls for reform from Republicans two years ago, we would not be at this crisis point today. We're proud that our advocacy has finally made a difference, but it's bittersweet, knowing that these reforms could have saved people from abuse if enacted earlier," Carnevale concluded.

### ###

name: JusticeDemocrats.com
subject: $100,000 by February 1
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

First, we want to say THANK YOU for the incredible outpouring of
grassroots support over the past 24 hours since we formally launched our
2026 Candidate Recruitment campaign.

So far, we’ve already raised $44,754 from emails like this one to help us
hire an additional staff member and secure the resources needed to recruit
as many working-class candidates for Congress as possible — but we’ve
still got a ways to go to reach our $100,000 goal by February 1.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250115-1?refcode=em20250115-36324&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=36324_1203867_lZZ_We&akid=36324%2E1203867%2ElZZ_We ]Will you rush a contribution of ANY amount right now to chip away at
our goal?

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250115-1?refcode=em20250115-36324&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=36324_1203867_lZZ_We&akid=36324%2E1203867%2ElZZ_We ]2026 Candidate Recruitment Fund. Raised: $44,754. Goal: $100,000.

Too often, it’s millionaires, Wall Street swindlers, and corporate
executives running for office to craft policy and pass laws that will
boost their profits rather than meet the needs of everyday people.

We’ve been saying it for years: we need more working-class members of
Congress to represent working-class voters.

But it takes a ton of resources to do this work, especially with lobby
groups, super PACs, and billionaire families spending more money than ever
before (over $4.5 billion in the 2024 cycle) to defeat progressives and
win elections to protect their profits.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250115-1?refcode=em20250115-36324&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36324_1203867_lZZ_We&akid=36324%2E1203867%2ElZZ_We ]Justice Democrats is focused on finding and supporting working-class
candidates to run in 2026 and stand up to corporate lobbies, billionaire
CEOs, and MAGA fascism. Will you help us by pitching in $5
or more now?

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250115-1?refcode=em20250115-36324&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36324_1203867_lZZ_We&akid=36324%2E1203867%2ElZZ_We ]Contribute $5

Thanks for anything you can contribute — every dollar matters as we rush
to raise $100,000 before the month ends.

— Justice Democrats

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20250115-36324-footer&t=1002&refcode2=36324_1203867_lZZ_We&akid=36324%2E1203867%2ElZZ_We

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1003&akid=36324%2E1203867%2ElZZ_We

name: Rudy Giuliani
subject: President Trump: “Save Rudy!”
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rudy Giuliani Freedom Fund Legal Defense Trust
body:
The trial of my life begins this week. And I don’t get a jury!
| [](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=3a7a2ef6d3ff4f898446eedd78e8b8a8&_e=hT24LCdLaJpi9vS0RbF8j3UAyRDB_5FCuH8H6nRrxAYtbj1kD3i2ygDktMIFWTbCoKR3GIcD9oUPT9epanEYXyffNJYq6_SUJWE5QfaNasuvMgdfPS6EkSTTLhq27bylrkFA0K-bvFMQwSxWkp2aButs02pTGowFhCFkHVSFVhpfbi2pBjd22AQCpkaq1wGvygvsLxN3gTL2fxLPz6btAfX2plQrRPN6NC5-DmBfXmpvDJjBiTP4xb41qbr8MxoTxaA6R5HJe7dJLE4pC8qvddl0FB5rQ5FIc5ipWSPllD1_hiDbGWVthGhroEv2lKEqEJdKDt2qn6RPRDefo_5fM8J3FM7UfKISYJz-iEn4Dw9jmuRmAPYBdxtL--A-MkqM)
---
Fellow Patriot,
---
With the trial of my life set to begin, President Donald J. Trump just said two powerful words for the entire nation to hear...
---
**_“SAVE RUDY!”_**
---
On Thursday, I will be on trial – __without_ a jury, mind you_ – to determine whether or not I still have a home to live in. It’s a trial to save my home and a trial to fight for my basic freedoms and rights.
---
_But more than that, our nation’s very system of justice will also be on trial._
---
If the Radical Left Democrats can get away with destroying me in a weaponized court without a jury – and possibly imprisoning me – solely because I served as President Trump’s lawyer, then the rule of law is DEAD in America.
---
**Just last week, a bloodthirsty left-wing judge threatened me with contempt and imprisonment if I didn’t recite her dictated false version of covered-up massive evidence of voter fraud.**
---
I had a choice to make: _I could recite the state-sanctioned propaganda like an obedient little dog and be rewarded with a pat on the head. Or, I could have the courage to stand for what I know to be true._
---
When I remind you of this Bible verse, you’ll know exactly which choice I made: "_For what shall it profit a man, if he shall gain the whole world, and lose his own soul?_ " (Mark 8:36)
---
You see, I refuse to give up on America. That’s why I’m going on trial to fight to save my home, my liberty, and __our__ Republic.
---
_Are YOU with me?_ Will you join the cause to “SAVE RUDY?”
---

Ritchie Torres represents the future of the Democratic Party. Raised by a single mother in public housing, Ritchie personally understands the hardships that working families endure. At age 25, Ritchie made history as the youngest elected official to serve on the New York City Council. He fought tirelessly for the working families of The Bronx, and now, he's fighting for those families and for progressive values in Congress. He is the first openly gay Afro Latino ever elected to Congress. Ritchie is a champion for progressive policies to fight poverty and to improve the lives of families just like the one he was raised in.

* **TOP NEWS**

"**Rep. Dustin Burrows voted Texas House speaker in blow to insurgent GOP movement,** " Texas Tribune's**James Barragan** ,**Jasper Scherer** ,**Renzo Downey** and **Robert Downen** -- "Rep. **Dustin Burrows** , R-Lubbock, was elected speaker of the Texas House on Tuesday, concluding a bitter and intense power struggle within the Republican Party that came down to an extraordinary floor vote to decide who would fill one of the most powerful positions in state government.

In his acceptance speech, Burrows focused on his desire to keep the House an independent institution, where lawmakers could feel free to disagree with one another without fear of repercussion.

"This is the people's House," said Burrows, who was joined by his wife Elisabeth and three sons for his swearing-in ceremony.

He promised to work with every member of the Legislature, leaving his door open even to those who opposed him.

"I commit to you today, every member will have a voice," he said. "Every district will have a seat at the table."

Burrows' win marks a disappointing defeat for the insurgent wing of the party, which has spent years working to wrest control of the lower chamber away from establishment Republicans. That faction, which aims to push the House further to the right, ousted more than a dozen GOP members in last year's primaries who were aligned with Burrows and his predecessor, Rep. **Dade Phelan** of Beaumont, riding a wave of conservative backlash over the House's impeachment of Attorney General **Ken Paxton** and failure to pass a private school voucher bill.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/a8688435-7fba-44f7-b6cd-a77ac806ed1a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
After the record turnover, hardline conservatives saw this year's speaker race as perhaps their best chance ever to depose the current House leadership. They selected as their standard-bearer Rep. **David Cook** of Mansfield, who vowed to follow through on insurgent demands to strip power from Democrats -- including barring them from chairing committees -- and weaken the speaker's means of controlling the chamber.

But while the insurgent crowd succeeded in forcing Phelan to give up the gavel, the elevation of his close ally signals a similar power structure will likely remain in place.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/a8688435-7fba-44f7-b6cd-a77ac806ed1a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Burrows won the speaker's race after two rounds of voting, edging out Cook by a vote of 85-55. During the first round, Rep. **Ana-Mar ia Rodriguez-Ramos**, a Richardson Democrat, received 23 votes from fellow Democratic members -- enough to keep Burrows and Cook from reaching the majority threshold of 76.

In the ensuing runoff round, twelve Democrats who had voted for Rodriguez-Ramos switched to Burrows. He also netted two more GOP votes from members who defected from Cook. Nine members marked themselves present without voting for either option, and one member, **Yvonne Davis** of Dallas, was marked as absent.
Burrows' winning coalition was made up of 49 Democrats and 36 Republicans, drawing instant backlash from hardline members and activists who vowed revenge in next year's primaries.

"Today, the RINO (anti-MAGA) Establishment laughed in the faces of our Republican grassroots. They chose Democrat chairs and Lobbyist buddies over their voters," Rep. **Shelley Luther** , a freshman Republican from Tom Bean who had vowed to oppose the Phelan-Burrows leadership team, [wrote on social media](https://substack.com/redirect/3c36907f-3331-4d22-8e34-98d0fa1b5680?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). "I look forward to seeing if they're still laughing after the next round of primaries. The fight starts today."

Burrows' support from Democrats will likely become major ammunition for his Republican critics aligned with the party's right wing who warned that the new speaker should be chosen by a majority of GOP votes. On Tuesday, conservative activists sent buses full of Cook supporters to the Capitol to urge their lawmakers to support their preferred candidate.

Activists wearing red shirts that read "Ban Democrat Chairs" and "Betrayed by Dade" referencing Phelan filled the halls of the Capitol.

Paxton, a top state Republican leader who had [traveled the state urging people to pressure their lawmakers to vote for Cook](https://substack.com/redirect/ef1478d3-9b42-48ec-b5aa-416b0c9ad699?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), criticized Burrows for winning the speaker's gavel with Democrat support and for going against Cook who had been the House Republican Caucus' endorsed nominee. He called on Burrows to prove his conservative bona fides and warned that he would keep fighting to "reform the Texas House."

"It is now important that Speaker Burrows and his leadership team pass every conservative legislative priority in a timely manner to make our state more secure and more prosperous," he said. "That is what Texas Republicans expect."

Lt. Gov. **Dan Patrick** , an archconservative who presides over the Senate, was also critical of Burrows' reliance on Democrat votes to win the speaker's race. He said he expected Burrows to help the House pass legislation supported by social conservatives including: school vouchers, banning cities and counties from hiring lobbyists to advocate for them in Austin, bail reform, election integrity bills, putting the Ten Commandments in school classrooms, banning the teaching of Critical Race Theory in the state's colleges and universities and banning people from countries that are adversaries of the United States from owning land in Texas.

"Each of these bills will be passed again by the Texas Senate," Patrick said in a statement. "The voters will hold our new speaker accountable to keep his promise of being the most conservative speaker in Texas history."" ($5/mo or $50/yr or $199 lifetime) TOP NEWS "Rep. Dustin Burrows voted Texas House speaker in blow to insurgent GOP movement, " Texas Tribune'sJames Barragan ,Jasper Scherer ,Renzo Downey and Robert Downen -- "Rep. Dustin Burrows , R-Lubbock, was elected speaker of the Texas House on Tuesday, concluding a bitter and intense power struggle within the Republican Party that came down to an extraordinary floor vote to decide who would fill one of the most powerful positions in state government. In his acceptance speech, Burrows focused on his desire to keep the House an independent institution, where lawmakers could feel free to disagree with one another without fear of repercussion. "This is the people's House," said Burrows, who was joined by his wife Elisabeth and three sons for his swearing-in ceremony. He promised to work with every member of the Legislature, leaving his door open even to those who opposed him. "I commit to you today, every member will have a voice," he said. "Every district will have a seat at the table." Burrows' win marks a disappointing defeat for the insurgent wing of the party, which has spent years working to wrest control of the lower chamber away from establishment Republicans. That faction, which aims to push the House further to the right, ousted more than a dozen GOP members in last year's primaries who were aligned with Burrows and his predecessor, Rep. Dade Phelan of Beaumont, riding a wave of conservative backlash over the House's impeachment of Attorney General Ken Paxton and failure to pass a private school voucher bill. After the record turnover, hardline conservatives saw this year's speaker race as perhaps their best chance ever to depose the current House leadership. They selected as their standard-bearer Rep. David Cook of Mansfield, who vowed to follow through on insurgent demands to strip power from Democrats -- including barring them from chairing committees -- and weaken the speaker's means of controlling the chamber. But while the insurgent crowd succeeded in forcing Phelan to give up the gavel, the elevation of his close ally signals a similar power structure will likely remain in place. Burrows won the speaker's race after two rounds of voting, edging out Cook by a vote of 85-55. During the first round, Rep. Ana-Mar ia Rodriguez-Ramos, a Richardson Democrat, received 23 votes from fellow Democratic members -- enough to keep Burrows and Cook from reaching the majority threshold of 76. In the ensuing runoff round, twelve Democrats who had voted for Rodriguez-Ramos switched to Burrows. He also netted two more GOP votes from members who defected from Cook. Nine members marked themselves present without voting for either option, and one member, Yvonne Davis of Dallas, was marked as absent. Burrows' winning coalition was made up of 49 Democrats and 36 Republicans, drawing instant backlash from hardline members and activists who vowed revenge in next year's primaries. "Today, the RINO (anti-MAGA) Establishment laughed in the faces of our Republican grassroots. They chose Democrat chairs and Lobbyist buddies over their voters," Rep. Shelley Luther , a freshman Republican from Tom Bean who had vowed to oppose the Phelan-Burrows leadership team, . "I look forward to seeing if they're still laughing after the next round of primaries. The fight starts today." Burrows' support from Democrats will likely become major ammunition for his Republican critics aligned with the party's right wing who warned that the new speaker should be chosen by a majority of GOP votes. On Tuesday, conservative activists sent buses full of Cook supporters to the Capitol to urge their lawmakers to support their preferred candidate. Activists wearing red shirts that read "Ban Democrat Chairs" and "Betrayed by Dade" referencing Phelan filled the halls of the Capitol. Paxton, a top state Republican leader who had , criticized Burrows for winning the speaker's gavel with Democrat support and for going against Cook who had been the House Republican Caucus' endorsed nominee. He called on Burrows to prove his conservative bona fides and warned that he would keep fighting to "reform the Texas House." "It is now important that Speaker Burrows and his leadership team pass every conservative legislative priority in a timely manner to make our state more secure and more prosperous," he said. "That is what Texas Republicans expect." Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick , an archconservative who presides over the Senate, was also critical of Burrows' reliance on Democrat votes to win the speaker's race. He said he expected Burrows to help the House pass legislation supported by social conservatives including: school vouchers, banning cities and counties from hiring lobbyists to advocate for them in Austin, bail reform, election integrity bills, putting the Ten Commandments in school classrooms, banning the teaching of Critical Race Theory in the state's colleges and universities and banning people from countries that are adversaries of the United States from owning land in Texas. "Each of these bills will be passed again by the Texas Senate," Patrick said in a statement. "The voters will hold our new speaker accountable to keep his promise of being the most conservative speaker in Texas history."" Patriot,

Congratulations to President-Elect Trump and Vice President-Elect Vance!

_I am honored to be in Washington, D.C., fighting to restore the America First agenda so that every family and working person can succeed in the United States again._

The American people have spoken loud and clear--they are ready for:

🇺🇸 A thriving economy
🇺🇸 A secure border
🇺🇸 Energy independence and dominance
🇺🇸 Safer streets and stronger communities
🇺🇸 America reclaiming its leadership on the global stage

Thank you!

* **TOP NEWS**

“**Rep. Dustin Burrows voted Texas House speaker in blow to insurgent GOP movement,** ” Texas Tribune's**James Barragan** ,**Jasper Scherer** ,**Renzo Downey** and **Robert Downen** — “Rep. **Dustin Burrows** , R-Lubbock, was elected speaker of the Texas House on Tuesday, concluding a bitter and intense power struggle within the Republican Party that came down to an extraordinary floor vote to decide who would fill one of the most powerful positions in state government.

In his acceptance speech, Burrows focused on his desire to keep the House an independent institution, where lawmakers could feel free to disagree with one another without fear of repercussion.

“This is the people’s House,” said Burrows, who was joined by his wife Elisabeth and three sons for his swearing-in ceremony.

He promised to work with every member of the Legislature, leaving his door open even to those who opposed him.

“I commit to you today, every member will have a voice,” he said. “Every district will have a seat at the table.”

Burrows’ win marks a disappointing defeat for the insurgent wing of the party, which has spent years working to wrest control of the lower chamber away from establishment Republicans. That faction, which aims to push the House further to the right, ousted more than a dozen GOP members in last year’s primaries who were aligned with Burrows and his predecessor, Rep. **Dade Phelan** of Beaumont, riding a wave of conservative backlash over the House’s impeachment of Attorney General **Ken Paxton** and failure to pass a private school voucher bill.
» [BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.IpGzAqnc3m6dMmKQdod4OdZdsfX-sjnRfDuEmaXW-5lN-IqpP1xu2_Ygevf9CBs8iGkDwdDuKv-OIdRCcryDDgoqnwbBfsyGSk_4VJrOSCvmgxZEZTEXv72w0U68JXHpny88FhFoE4mM3a9phL8bbCZnH0MVVOeL5UxA_WkdG5B-dfHv5q1IYzucwXD6Ty9CvBQfcfgBo5j99TceDZwPG_JANyVeVywGYpGasnmZ8iogWdFOUaU3-FiWY8YSpMbVsEVLaaqgW8Oi34YEaatJLp4R4BQ9-Nm5L3e9IIj46wVP6ElSl7CPfzcyS2XtQlJfxXiB1rZCgD9kxrDV0zUAmwePaVe34JOdMgZFAY-Lm7YBs_PkwYykTTlsv3Qfl8_4Hvv6kuJ9YyfSHlZwl4U_ITneRU7lIFn9Ze7TGKciyySU047bf_MbJ4fdMMa8pbrECyNoZLNxurIAsDrWju-mRg/4d6/LCaQnGXITKaCwUrJWD8SEg/h14/h001.SkgOh6fYn9lRGWQ7SaPBuzgaB2gwT-lXq6I_K9IxQWU)
After the record turnover, hardline conservatives saw this year’s speaker race as perhaps their best chance ever to depose the current House leadership. They selected as their standard-bearer Rep. **David Cook** of Mansfield, who vowed to follow through on insurgent demands to strip power from Democrats — including barring them from chairing committees — and weaken the speaker’s means of controlling the chamber.

But while the insurgent crowd succeeded in forcing Phelan to give up the gavel, the elevation of his close ally signals a similar power structure will likely remain in place.
» [BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.IpGzAqnc3m6dMmKQdod4OdZdsfX-sjnRfDuEmaXW-5lN-IqpP1xu2_Ygevf9CBs8iGkDwdDuKv-OIdRCcryDDgoqnwbBfsyGSk_4VJrOSCvmgxZEZTEXv72w0U68JXHpny88FhFoE4mM3a9phL8bbCZnH0MVVOeL5UxA_WkdG5B-dfHv5q1IYzucwXD6Ty9CvBQfcfgBo5j99TceDZwPG_JANyVeVywGYpGasnmZ8iogWdFOUaU3-FiWY8YSpMbVsEVLaaqgW8Oi34YEaatJLp4R4BQ9-Nm5L3e9IIj46wVP6ElSl7CPfzcyS2XtQlJfxXiB1rZCgD9kxrDV0zUAmwePaVe34JOdMgZFAY-Lm7YBs_PkwYykTTlsv3Qfl8_4Hvv6kuJ9YyfSHlZwl4U_IQKfpKSbWEiY78QfGXM_bF_tOp8Otz8tpMhShwk5g1cJJ6422tKoqSd02Y24DRuNTw/4d6/LCaQnGXITKaCwUrJWD8SEg/h15/h001.xwmWn5UPAPuPkMw6PTQelrQ7HLI26u_M7Guj9ifLpeQ)
Burrows won the speaker’s race after two rounds of voting, edging out Cook by a vote of 85-55. During the first round, Rep. **Ana-María Rodríguez-Ramos** , a Richardson Democrat, received 23 votes from fellow Democratic members — enough to keep Burrows and Cook from reaching the majority threshold of 76.

In the ensuing runoff round, twelve Democrats who had voted for Rodríguez-Ramos switched to Burrows. He also netted two more GOP votes from members who defected from Cook. Nine members marked themselves present without voting for either option, and one member, **Yvonne Davis** of Dallas, was marked as absent.
Burrows’ winning coalition was made up of 49 Democrats and 36 Republicans, drawing instant backlash from hardline members and activists who vowed revenge in next year’s primaries.

“Today, the RINO (anti-MAGA) Establishment laughed in the faces of our Republican grassroots. They chose Democrat chairs and Lobbyist buddies over their voters,” Rep. **Shelley Luther** , a freshman Republican from Tom Bean who had vowed to oppose the Phelan-Burrows leadership team, [wrote on social media](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.Tb7ejk-5QfVnOrZFvpt2rnFWxZC39t9i8BwAavg8bPdpt_Wb1kBfc4LlpSZ5NCtWTr64XQX78jlwQVI9Osi0mVfD7rPKnBs9MxQ-vsV_tCVYBfiKodx0esLy8o2TbDBadmEF3_5T4ZvPeQ596NKFXWYwMTuiKKy9fU_lJYfrgR_XHcudYDdQPRJlIxDahft1u9M7m73q9z20_RB2eGAozdyAY0IMb0i00_6luhE5WF-wqXDmycJCALnnfcPGr7JyemW-7PCKyqeRz7gIfyovkb1-4vH58gJRPhQJouEGLxjJdVK6vqBKKLeESHu2CNsO441qywKVgMxch4C8r-SWrynfUXpHzZNtPgrdBFhhBNSQjxmbB4L92YuT0VEpJtziDWwmUe64oFAPnSbsEeSnm2sGAL_rDrvTcLsBKM9ERtygWnR5313lOhkzg3aiUV0AnpeoBoafXj4yuvVRLGAczdk0nLSdcqC5zACO_sazRcuB8utjr-39OrVjmxlKa3Vy/4d6/LCaQnGXITKaCwUrJWD8SEg/h16/h001.Wz3e712lo2mNdfzSkC6d-MsXoSADoCCwAx-mpCIKDxs). “I look forward to seeing if they’re still laughing after the next round of primaries. The fight starts today.”

Burrows’ support from Democrats will likely become major ammunition for his Republican critics aligned with the party’s right wing who warned that the new speaker should be chosen by a majority of GOP votes. On Tuesday, conservative activists sent buses full of Cook supporters to the Capitol to urge their lawmakers to support their preferred candidate.

Activists wearing red shirts that read “Ban Democrat Chairs” and “Betrayed by Dade” referencing Phelan filled the halls of the Capitol.

Paxton, a top state Republican leader who had [traveled the state urging people to pressure their lawmakers to vote for Cook](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eCyDm7YZItPaA-BvJlf4tsBKrql82Q6Mdf028qH0lEg8xUuVE6m-VyGOSfC5VsDtZMN4P718JYX2aTTOm5kO1feepE8l-ez3N8NRxkMnMVqAPJYQf2LcSZmoNl430cUT0lNQWgOu4DjkkhtUX9biedKEkEOFgdCGtxZMSyuqf8_zzhOmjE3NyPtHMsoyuTGakLGSmq6-4klc43jQK70OA0LLZaiaAhkqog-7aIt8kPQw5jRQdkaee09NZlhzAg37-IYqlm_7-8FLB8GivlMoQ_QS9CyI8gJ0ZZZEEmkbN5JLV0gkLqh4ouyNI-FcHso8rjzR_4EL9J8O821lEMNBqCf2Lk3ERVOQAISwjh8PbRbpkocgDRq2WJOr1_GReEVR7vucIsiMK2mk320CiBs15anzn1CZcuBaEboy3in-lMUcExrThnNHkfF95LhLzzYB1J2_Kx9IISSbSDfYnJ81Mgy5peFrUKb9ySFo0XXW_VrkVRfJ3ILh5jkXc-5gJXv7Bqwb5_akx5SE-z99n5MVEco/4d6/LCaQnGXITKaCwUrJWD8SEg/h17/h001.xRcVLZ9Jc4CMtm3Z00RM-70uz66SnEOiJGGT8poOA_Q), criticized Burrows for winning the speaker’s gavel with Democrat support and for going against Cook who had been the House Republican Caucus’ endorsed nominee. He called on Burrows to prove his conservative bona fides and warned that he would keep fighting to “reform the Texas House.”

“It is now important that Speaker Burrows and his leadership team pass every conservative legislative priority in a timely manner to make our state more secure and more prosperous,” he said. “That is what Texas Republicans expect.”

Lt. Gov. **Dan Patrick** , an archconservative who presides over the Senate, was also critical of Burrows’ reliance on Democrat votes to win the speaker’s race. He said he expected Burrows to help the House pass legislation supported by social conservatives including: school vouchers, banning cities and counties from hiring lobbyists to advocate for them in Austin, bail reform, election integrity bills, putting the Ten Commandments in school classrooms, banning the teaching of Critical Race Theory in the state’s colleges and universities and banning people from countries that are adversaries of the United States from owning land in Texas.

“Each of these bills will be passed again by the Texas Senate,” Patrick said in a statement. “The voters will hold our new speaker accountable to keep his promise of being the most conservative speaker in Texas history.”" [Texas Tribune](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eCyDm7YZItPaA-BvJlf4tsDAnSXIR4hdrR41zr3sHTvKjTxjIj-X16hGmg7z5vcXDe4vAOyWtT8l07EpFrNlMapm7gHz0RikO8L-JCxODJOxXVuy5c7rdJzxcuhMLCe_pPg4PdS1GzG523Tj3PAPtQlKVD7ZVDpgo7Mw4w6DlyyYc6CUJCR8aQUEFRLawUDGiVj6mwybI18Wh2UFQIWM-P44yiaeZ-r20Enl_mBziwkfP1oCWJ_f3_fWH1MluR-vz6Y9ToAIh5sFaoo014TtomfaB3pvcruLTK9-ruTy2IGoqr4mogYjSFHpPhzX0nPisllgdE6NW_qJFHRqHWINsHi1EDfVG8U6tUUiixMaokXbNDyaD14_yecdqPOAP5iVfEwKuWsGjXEUS8ifURHBgWc5Ia8g-MFPMBCZJrMb7LxnQOGS_Q_OP8XakmLVr1I9HFcbaGE8XJUxEcsgTJsF5QjJhqFts3RHIgYr-1sYWLMkC6pFJkpvQLTlyZheOcG__tAJZKMwHHjva-Aj_K_N_0o/4d6/LCaQnGXITKaCwUrJWD8SEg/h18/h001.TJfl95OsjwnJPjn1oyjiy_9hJ4sCflCaSjUstztSvDA)

Don't miss out on the fastest-growing daily newsletter in Texas – join a thriving community that values accurate, relevant news delivered straight to your inbox every morning. | TOP NEWS “Rep. Dustin Burrows voted Texas House speaker in blow to insurgent GOP movement, ” Texas Tribune'sJames Barragan ,Jasper Scherer ,Renzo Downey and Robert Downen — “Rep. Dustin Burrows , R-Lubbock, was elected speaker of the Texas House on Tuesday, concluding a bitter and intense power struggle within the Republican Party that came down to an extraordinary floor vote to decide who would fill one of the most powerful positions in state government. In his acceptance speech, Burrows focused on his desire to keep the House an independent institution, where lawmakers could feel free to disagree with one another without fear of repercussion. “This is the people’s House,” said Burrows, who was joined by his wife Elisabeth and three sons for his swearing-in ceremony. He promised to work with every member of the Legislature, leaving his door open even to those who opposed him. “I commit to you today, every member will have a voice,” he said. “Every district will have a seat at the table.” Burrows’ win marks a disappointing defeat for the insurgent wing of the party, which has spent years working to wrest control of the lower chamber away from establishment Republicans. That faction, which aims to push the House further to the right, ousted more than a dozen GOP members in last year’s primaries who were aligned with Burrows and his predecessor, Rep. Dade Phelan of Beaumont, riding a wave of conservative backlash over the House’s impeachment of Attorney General Ken Paxton and failure to pass a private school voucher bill. » After the record turnover, hardline conservatives saw this year’s speaker race as perhaps their best chance ever to depose the current House leadership. They selected as their standard-bearer Rep. David Cook of Mansfield, who vowed to follow through on insurgent demands to strip power from Democrats — including barring them from chairing committees — and weaken the speaker’s means of controlling the chamber. But while the insurgent crowd succeeded in forcing Phelan to give up the gavel, the elevation of his close ally signals a similar power structure will likely remain in place. » Burrows won the speaker’s race after two rounds of voting, edging out Cook by a vote of 85-55. During the first round, Rep. Ana-María Rodríguez-Ramos , a Richardson Democrat, received 23 votes from fellow Democratic members — enough to keep Burrows and Cook from reaching the majority threshold of 76. In the ensuing runoff round, twelve Democrats who had voted for Rodríguez-Ramos switched to Burrows. He also netted two more GOP votes from members who defected from Cook. Nine members marked themselves present without voting for either option, and one member, Yvonne Davis of Dallas, was marked as absent. Burrows’ winning coalition was made up of 49 Democrats and 36 Republicans, drawing instant backlash from hardline members and activists who vowed revenge in next year’s primaries. “Today, the RINO (anti-MAGA) Establishment laughed in the faces of our Republican grassroots. They chose Democrat chairs and Lobbyist buddies over their voters,” Rep. Shelley Luther , a freshman Republican from Tom Bean who had vowed to oppose the Phelan-Burrows leadership team, . “I look forward to seeing if they’re still laughing after the next round of primaries. The fight starts today.” Burrows’ support from Democrats will likely become major ammunition for his Republican critics aligned with the party’s right wing who warned that the new speaker should be chosen by a majority of GOP votes. On Tuesday, conservative activists sent buses full of Cook supporters to the Capitol to urge their lawmakers to support their preferred candidate. Activists wearing red shirts that read “Ban Democrat Chairs” and “Betrayed by Dade” referencing Phelan filled the halls of the Capitol. Paxton, a top state Republican leader who had , criticized Burrows for winning the speaker’s gavel with Democrat support and for going against Cook who had been the House Republican Caucus’ endorsed nominee. He called on Burrows to prove his conservative bona fides and warned that he would keep fighting to “reform the Texas House.” “It is now important that Speaker Burrows and his leadership team pass every conservative legislative priority in a timely manner to make our state more secure and more prosperous,” he said. “That is what Texas Republicans expect.” Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick , an archconservative who presides over the Senate, was also critical of Burrows’ reliance on Democrat votes to win the speaker’s race. He said he expected Burrows to help the House pass legislation supported by social conservatives including: school vouchers, banning cities and counties from hiring lobbyists to advocate for them in Austin, bail reform, election integrity bills, putting the Ten Commandments in school classrooms, banning the teaching of Critical Race Theory in the state’s colleges and universities and banning people from countries that are adversaries of the United States from owning land in Texas. “Each of these bills will be passed again by the Texas Senate,” Patrick said in a statement. “The voters will hold our new speaker accountable to keep his promise of being the most conservative speaker in Texas history.”" | FREE SUBSCRIPTIONS HAVE ENDED : As of July 11, 2022, we have moved to a paywall for free subscribers. name: Sean Feucht
subject: Wildfire Relief ROUND TWO
body:
| | | [](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=dcac8242330e4c15a55d3b915d5debd3&_m=3254b81289264e88aabc9411e0db823b&_e=awiRPL4kFQqfHHtzX61IuF7SLoswoMVdj0DKUIl9R4_wC5q76pIRE_NyCEbPCwbbjgHMAXL90jcYiK71KlQStQSpFdbmbajRrtdNCYBmFsilWkBjFaycR3OpBfzrZUvSRUhFJvM-DgAXm5HK-xN-yfHM0KDY7g1HABfbNA1qqcUPvXg2qEz6_i-m-gRTBbaiiNEKkyF4m67LpcbPn7gzZvDThRBRG94bgdvmc5GZf7d7oPi_E7H6tTnsqF8i-4yRemGBgww4rOSIpGT54ZI8wDq-6yXvpNJ64etqf_DIynTdscqGDhvnYZ1ZzwR9RQcE)
---
Dear Friend,
---
When the church shows the love of Christ, the world takes notice. We put out a call for Believers to show the love of Christ to our brothers and sisters impacted by the California wildfires. And you stepped up in an eternal way.

Thanks to Believers from across the country, we were able to bless those impacted by the wildfires with prayer, aid and financial support. And we're asking you to do it again. Our friends at Fearless Church saw what happened at Angelus Temple and invited us to come bring in aid and minister to evacuees and fire fighters **TOMORROW NIGHT**.
---
[](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=dcac8242330e4c15a55d3b915d5debd3&_m=3254b81289264e88aabc9411e0db823b&_e=awiRPL4kFQqfHHtzX61IuF7SLoswoMVdj0DKUIl9R4_wC5q76pIRE_NyCEbPCwbbjgHMAXL90jcYiK71KlQStQSpFdbmbajRrtdNCYBmFsilWkBjFaycR3OpBfzrZUvSRUhFJvM-DgAXm5HK-xN-yfHM0KDY7g1HABfbNA1qqcXuisNUnNAxfgwvfxKnxNTbSvpZk0pHomCR-1Cqkv9M92HNO3_HGXJA4tOYMluwujUlkEZCiGEdsdC-43bsLFmQa0Tr2V-TIPd1jexKnKxELN7_8rjhkE1Sbd43eVSC5q_svFeSC8pAymntpBDVlSsz)
---
I can't give enough thanks to the countless Believers who have already donated to help bless the victims of the California wildfires. To those of you who haven't contributed, I'm asking that you partner with us to be the hands and feet of Jesus. And to those who have already donated and are in a position to do it again, I'm asking that you would. What we do here will have a lasting impact, an eternal impact on untold lives. If you are in the area, please bring the following supplies to help us minister to the evacuees and fire fighters. • **Gift cards **(Amazon, Walmart, Target, Gas, Retail Stores, Restaurants).• **Candles or calming essential oils** (chamomile, eucalyptus) for relaxation. • **Soothing teas** (chamomile, peppermint) and a pretty tea mug.• ** Cozy blankets** for comfort during stressful times. • **Spa products** such as bath salts, bath bombs, or a face mask to unwind.• **Personal notes** expressing your support and appreciation for their strength • **Scripture**.• **Art supplies** : crayons, markers, coloring books, and craft kits for creative expression. • **Stuffed animals** : or a comfort toy to provide emotional support• **Games** : simple board game (Uno, Connect 4) or card games for family playtime. • **Puzzles** to help children focus and pass the time while their parent is away.• **Children's books** about bravery, resilience, or the role of first responders. • **Outdoor toys** : sidewalk chalk, bubbles, or jump ropes for outdoor fun when weather permits.
---
Till all hear,
---
[**DONATE RELIEF SUPPLIES >>>**](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=dcac8242330e4c15a55d3b915d5debd3&_m=3254b81289264e88aabc9411e0db823b&_e=awiRPL4kFQqfHHtzX61IuF7SLoswoMVdj0DKUIl9R4_wC5q76pIRE_NyCEbPCwbbjgHMAXL90jcYiK71KlQStQSpFdbmbajRrtdNCYBmFsilWkBjFaycR3OpBfzrZUvSRUhFJvM-DgAXm5HK-xN-yfHM0KDY7g1HABfbNA1qqcXGaCdk2KRJN3jeJgX0HHoWSkSSYE-MXcfQB6lDpT8aNdvv3sSOUeUAWvYyh5I0yEmc2wrY-sqqjTYDUDDN-JOQSh-Jdu8E8q0Yuv_wu7Cersw5kGpEJUK6wjNULPWad8SYQ-3ZCIs-ir-uVIrtVQRP)
---
---
Sean
---
[**DONATE RELIEF SUPPLIES >>>**](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=dcac8242330e4c15a55d3b915d5debd3&_m=3254b81289264e88aabc9411e0db823b&_e=awiRPL4kFQqfHHtzX61IuF7SLoswoMVdj0DKUIl9R4_wC5q76pIRE_NyCEbPCwbbjgHMAXL90jcYiK71KlQStQSpFdbmbajRrtdNCYBmFsilWkBjFaycR3OpBfzrZUvSRUhFJvM-DgAXm5HK-xN-yfHM0KDY7g1HABfbNA1qqcU0AfMK-fOlq_o7gj41JjTmTyK1dJLEQ57jYxD0UwXaBqHucQ0zd2tcvhWUnNKwxQ6L4hFWMQVf1O4PVuSaOSVuWvctGRSWaQj6a_Hoap6bLW-5NIH_ekOc_hVSklzfoT-Lt8a5LlEaI0z9u_xr1KIa)
---
|
---
| | **INTERNATIONAL DONORS— DONATE[HERE](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=dcac8242330e4c15a55d3b915d5debd3&_m=3254b81289264e88aabc9411e0db823b&_e=awiRPL4kFQqfHHtzX61IuFVxyi-yXMx58WOdf_ZT9utYu1Zebic7tBnf5LRUx-MmeTN8zBbpCBPwA9hLFQB2SraFnGIOUY3B3GMqQma5XOrZBc1-kgB0JGWScntrp3rVdWTAbvpWwUd0lMKt6-QXsMRA5r8M5stel1OMHsNaLtdRTS0txTVwLpmvjPxgwWwCPY4rIXziUHU3go3iZVo9uxrtj8IFtMvb9k_Imr0kH6xo3XoY3CXNr1z2Dyi9aSSUz1sF9cPYELZXtK7LbGSeu9Z27WFGP3vTqg46FXjMMxLV2sGBN3Wv8Qj6v0uBNjpX)** **Donate by Check or Money Order** Sean Feucht Ministries310 Third Street NEWashington, DC 20002 _Contributions are tax-deductible._

subject: TUNE IN - State of the Commonwealth Response Tomorrow 1/16
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Massachusetts Republican Party Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:

---
| Hi Peter,
This Thursday, January 16th, at MassGOP Headquarters, Representative Marcus Vaughn will take the podium to give the State of the Commonwealth Response around 7:30-8pm. The second annual Response will highlight how the Healey Administration and the Democratic Supermajority on Beacon Hill have failed to address the housing affordability crisis and migrant catastrophe that are crushing the state.
---
| |
---
| Vaughn seeks to highlight a new path forward for Massachusetts residents. The event will be broadcast on social media, so please tune in [here](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001MXPluTdaqGlb-MjdPTXV3Fj7oGqM7ARQc0mlOx51OoLgO2BkB_GATTFI-fHBUUXsKr4DyFcLfYVAw9sLJ0b14MSrQ26AF96wWmlvBsVNbbAsXxZFhBYaRgJ-3EMbzoxfvy5nAhEuweNJIK1PvKc_wQ==&c=XAHp-QP5zSjuY81CRA_Z7xQYbw34E1zCY2NN1C0L0iwrZlzFkEOKtg==&ch=FydrNGPijmQwZtaUGpqUNBsogRWnUlOlpIz6M12dOxgJxjkIxK36Sg==) to watch the speech in real time.
The message will be made clear to the Democratic Supermajority - a truly viable two-party system in Massachusetts is no longer a pipe dream & they must put partisanship aside to work with Republicans on finding real solutions to the problems plaguing our state.
It takes courage and grit to stand up for Massachusetts. Let's show Marcus he has an army of support behind him -> [https://secure.winred.com/massachusetts-gop/sotcr](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001MXPluTdaqGlb-MjdPTXV3Fj7oGqM7ARQc0mlOx51OoLgO2BkB_GATU2QP0HlQX-NLHI5rRdcDHyaSLLYfNZi_ALE9tnFGRunNP8LgPpN--wKRC5VXdmA6zvZCbrnDplDmbX_OVwWphdy8DwW1ZqoEHllXA-uj4bSTdcviJdNN82YuV5u0zVdSeDktwCFtZPj&c=XAHp-QP5zSjuY81CRA_Z7xQYbw34E1zCY2NN1C0L0iwrZlzFkEOKtg==&ch=FydrNGPijmQwZtaUGpqUNBsogRWnUlOlpIz6M12dOxgJxjkIxK36Sg==)

name: Martin Heinrich
subject: We can’t start 2025 at a disadvantage
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Martin Heinrich for Senate
body:
These experiences motivated my work, and your support will help me continue it [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100443499/522782110/-272948131?refcode=AU_MTH_FR_GEN_MMG_AC_20250115_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwY2Y1NTFiNS02YWQzLWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=zSTIOg98PpFx-cJXyEueLXILkywuf_IgS99VZAoS_WQ=&emci=8cb91baf-6ad3-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&emdi=0cf551b5-6ad3-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&ceid=252045)
---
I became a U.S. Senator to deliver lasting results for folks in New Mexico and across the country. But I didn’t always know I’d end up here. [_**Before I share more, will you pitch in any amount before my campaign’s mid-month deadline tonight? It’s our first one of the year and missing it would put us at an early disadvantage.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100443505/522782118/226891441?amount=&refcode=AU_MTH_FR_GEN_MMG_AC_20250115_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwY2Y1NTFiNS02YWQzLWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=zSTIOg98PpFx-cJXyEueLXILkywuf_IgS99VZAoS_WQ=) | [DONATE NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100443510/522782123/226891441?amount=&refcode=AU_MTH_FR_GEN_MMG_AC_20250115_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwY2Y1NTFiNS02YWQzLWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=zSTIOg98PpFx-cJXyEueLXILkywuf_IgS99VZAoS_WQ=)
---
My parents were a union lineman and an automobile wheel factory worker. I went on to study mechanical engineering in college and have held jobs from working on directed energy to serving as an AmeriCorps fellow and doing nonprofit work.
All of those jobs helped me become who I am today. Now, I have a job where I strive to help people live the best lives they can — from working to lower healthcare costs and strengthen our economy, to making sure the air we breathe and the lands we live on are protected for future generations.
That’s why your support matters so much. Every dollar you give to emails like this one powers my work to deliver for New Mexico.

clean_body: These experiences motivated my work, and your support will help me continue it I became a U.S. Senator to deliver lasting results for folks in New Mexico and across the country. But I didn’t always know I’d end up here. = | = My parents were a union lineman and an automobile wheel factory worker. I went on to study mechanical engineering in college and have held jobs from working on directed energy to serving as an AmeriCorps fellow and doing nonprofit work. All of those jobs helped me become who I am today. Now, I have a job where I strive to help people live the best lives they can — from working to lower healthcare costs and strengthen our economy, to making sure the air we breathe and the lands we live on are protected for future generations. That’s why your support matters so much. name: The Democrats
body:
Take a look at this:
---
Take a look at this number, Derek: [](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-january-2025?refcode=em_20250115_2574_fr&amount=21.87&refcode2=2574_22930333_FN7PCX&akid=2574%2E22930333%2EFN7PCX)
That’s the average amount people are chipping in online to support the Democratic Party this year.
A _huge_ amount of the DNC’s work is funded by contributions like these — from people like you in Cresco — pitching in $5, $10, or $21.87 to help elect Democrats up and down the ballot.
Today’s the day we’re hoping you’ll join your fellow Democrats and make your first $21.87 contribution to the DNC this year, ahead of tonight’s mid-month fundraising deadline. Here’s why:
At midnight, we’ll close the books on our very last fundraising deadline before Trump is inaugurated as our next president.
Over the weekend, one Republican senator predicted that as soon as Trump is sworn in, he’ll issue “a blizzard of executive orders.” That means we truly have to be prepared for anything. Not only do we need to build the resources to mobilize and organize against Trump and his allies’ Project 2025 agenda, but we need to put ourselves in the strongest possible position to elect as many Democrats as we can — Democrats who will check Trump’s power at all levels of government.
[**This work takes a massive number of resources, and it’s why we’re hoping we can count on you to match our $21.87 average online contribution before tonight’s deadline.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-january-2025?amount=21.87&refcode=em_20250115_2574_fr&refcode2=2574_22930333_FN7PCX&akid=2574%2E22930333%2EFN7PCX)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_
| | **[CONTRIBUTE $21.87](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-january-2025?express_lane=true&amount=21.87&refcode=em_20250115_2574_fr&refcode2=2574_22930333_FN7PCX&akid=2574%2E22930333%2EFN7PCX)**
---
****
| **[CONTRIBUTE OTHER](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-january-2025?refcode=em_20250115_2574_fr&refcode2=2574_22930333_FN7PCX&akid=2574%2E22930333%2EFN7PCX)**
---
****
Look, we know it might not feel like $21.87 can make a huge impact, especially when Trump is backed by billionaires like Elon Musk. But trust us when we say these contributions add up fast.
Thank you in advance for doing your part to help,
The Democrats

_If you gave recently via check or mail, our system may not have yet processed your gift. We are appreciative and thank you greatly for your support._

clean_body: Right now, President Trump's nominees are facing the gauntlet... | | | | | Peter, Our mid-month deadline is TONIGHT at midnight, and we still haven’t hit our goal. The Democrat fundraising machine never slows down, and we need to keep pace with them! But there's still time, and I know we can make it. | | | | Right now, President Trump's nominees are facing the gauntlet— the Democrats are throwing every bit of rumor, hearsay, and downright slander that they can muster at the men and women tapped to help restore America. I'm fighting to help give the American people the agenda they voted for and to bring the kind of change that America so desperately needs. We must take action to secure the border, restore energy production, ensure the safety of the American people, remove criminals from our streets, and provide tax relief for working families. Peter, pitch in NOW before our huge deadline, and help DELIVER THE TRUMP AGENDA! | | Thank you, Josh Hawley | | | | | ---|---|---|--- Paid for by Josh Hawley Victory Committee

Peter, There are no off years when it comes to defending democracy. 2025 will bring legislative and governor’s races in Virginia and New Jersey, a critical supreme court race in Wisconsin, and three supreme court seats up in Pennsylvania. Those elections are vital opportunities to support candidates who will fight for fair maps and protect our freedoms. We know how to get things done even with Trump in office — because we’ve done it before. Our proven, tested strategy has generated real results.
Take, for example, how in 2016 the GOP won a sweeping 47-seat House majority — despite only narrowly winning the House popular vote. Trump used that significant majority to push through an extreme agenda.

name: Elissa Slotkin
subject: My first week as Senator
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ELISSA SLOTKIN FOR MICHIGAN
body:
# [](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/188?t=1&akid=2226%2E135344%2EVqQVBs)

Friend, I’ve officially completed my first week (and am now into week two) and it was jam-packed. I wanted to send a longer update your way, to explain a little bit more about what a Senator’s day-to-day life looks like -- from official business in DC to meeting with constituents in Michigan, we’re off to the races!
I spent the first half of the week in a very snowy Washington: first, I put on my snow gear and walked to the Capitol on Monday, January 6, and performed my constitutional duty as a member of the Senate and participated in the counting and certification of the 2024 Presidential election. I was keen to watch this typically perfunctory process since the only other time I tried to witness a certification was Jan 6, 2021, where I went running away from the Floor to barricade myself in my office.
I also got to attend proceedings honoring President Jimmy Carter, including an arrival ceremony in the Capitol Rotunda and his funeral at the National Cathedral -- and I cast my first vote on the floor of the Senate.
My days were also stuffed to the brim with meetings: with other Senators, as I continue to sit down with my colleagues from both sides of the aisle to deepen our relationships; with President Trump’s cabinet nominees, as I hear their plans to lead our critical agencies; and with industry leaders from Michigan -- whose input and partnership I will rely on as I represent our whole state.
I even found some time to give the DC press corps a hard time as they shivered in the snow -- 25 degrees and snowing is a normal winter day in Michigan!
Then in the second half of the week I went from the tradition and fanfare of the Senate to the hustle and bustle of Michigan.
There were more meetings, with local elected and community leaders at home who I’ll partner with over the next six years, and community festivals -- including the Fire and Ice Festival, a true taste of Michigan with fires, live ice carving, kid-friendly curling and (plastic) axe throwing.
Detroit’s Eastern Market was also a must-visit in my first week: I’ve been assigned to the Senate’s agriculture committee, and seeing the farmers and vendors at the market really brings home how essential Michigan farmers are to our economy.
And finally -- folks in Detroit know that the annual Auto Show is a huge deal and important tradition every year. We’re the state that builds cars, and the auto show is a showcase of the latest and greatest. It’s also a chance for leaders to talk about how to support the industry and Michigan’s workers, and even to support a good cause with their charity preview event (affectionately nicknamed “Auto Prom.”)
I can’t imagine a more perfect first week in the Senate, with a chance to perform my duties in Washington and visit with constituents back home.
I now head in to week two, ready to continue forming relationships, carrying out my voting duties, and this week — attending hearings with cabinet nominees, always asking how their work will impact Michiganders.
Thanks for following along -- and thanks for making this possible.
Elissa
* * *

👋🏻 **Wait, before you click away!** 🇺🇸
United States Senators often keep on fundraising after their election ends, but Elissa doesn't want to keep bombarding your inbox with fundraising requests.
What we will do is send you good, timely content - like this message. Stuff you might open without dread.
But she does hope you may consider making a small, monthly recurring contribution. Even just $5 or $10 - so we don't have to send those mass fundraising emails while Elissa is focused on her work as a Senator.
[**Make a recurring contribution to Elissa Slotkin's campaign here.**](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/1960?c0=ebs-em-jan25.mLp_Iw&refcode=em250115-1-xx&t=2&akid=2226%2E135344%2EVqQVBs)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your recurring contribution instantly:_
| | **[DONATE $10 MONTHLY](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/3243?c0=ebs-em-jan25.ld21Cu&refcode=em250115-1-xx&t=3&akid=2226%2E135344%2EVqQVBs)**
---
****
| **[DONATE OTHER](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/1960?c0=ebs-em-jan25.mLp_Iw&refcode=em250115-1-xx&t=4&akid=2226%2E135344%2EVqQVBs)**
---
****

PAID FOR BY ELISSA SLOTKIN FOR MICHIGAN
P.O. Box 4145
East Lansing, MI 48826
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a very important way we communicate with supporters like you, but if you want to, you can [ unsubscribe here](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=5&akid=2226%2E135344%2EVqQVBs).

[ Sign up here to receive fewer emails.](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/signup/users-fewer-emails/?t=6&akid=2226%2E135344%2EVqQVBs)

Elissa Slotkin served in the Central Intelligence Agency and the Department of Defense. Use of her job titles and photographs during service do not imply endorsement by the Central Intelligence Agency OR the Department of Defense.

name: [New Dems Petition Team]
subject: Will you sign our "Save Social Security" petition?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the New Democrat Coalition Action Fund
body:
| Hi team,

Once the Republican trifecta takes control of Congress and the White House, **they’ve threatened to go after Social Security protections that keep millions of Americans afloat.**

body:

| _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T486da827-e548-4e93-8a9e-6e4709cfc69b/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

| ONE-QUESTION POLL FOR dpwillis67@gmail.com: [](https://default.salsalabs.org/Ta1f19909-f9a9-47fc-a39a-932b5f762612/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) Will you watch Rachel Maddow on MSNBC? [[](https://default.salsalabs.org/Te770d32d-f83e-4046-aec6-59db20debdad/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)**[YES](https://default.salsalabs.org/T7dbf4c35-4406-42f9-ac7c-bf1fcffa4dba/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)****[→](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tb527181b-2fb7-4754-b219-ac915815383b/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)**[]](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tfb480aed-ac7d-4b93-8d9d-d5dd41b3f506/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) **[[](https://default.salsalabs.org/Ta31cca25-cf8c-4e48-ab8a-e5d6a0c688cd/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)[ NO ](https://default.salsalabs.org/T5fb9baa6-2c58-4ba2-a001-0536d7a0c916/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)[→](https://default.salsalabs.org/T42f97703-3979-4611-8ca5-9a0b9c623709/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)[]](https://default.salsalabs.org/T9ecec481-f061-48df-a646-b61d47333518/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)**
---
| Peter – MSNBC’s viewership has DROPPED OFF LIKE A CLIFF. Since the election, fewer and fewer people have watched cable news. But it’s one of the ways we’ve stayed up-to-date on events in our communities and throughout the world. If Trump had his way, though, MSNBC and other left-leaning news outlets would be kept in the dark. But thanks to folks like Rachel Maddow, they’re going to make sure they hold Trump accountable by going on the air – especially during the first 100 days of his administration. [In order to stop our news outlets from becoming Fox News, will you fill out this one-question poll to show how many Democrats will support Rachel Maddow and MSNBC once Trump is sworn in?](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tc280da8d-e39a-4ff5-bc0c-312d9ae2ca92/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! Please follow Cliff Schecter on Twitter @cliffschecter, or subscribe to The Takedown with Cliff Schecter on YouTube, to keep up to date with this battle for the heart and soul of our country. — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tbd5790dc-cf12-4b5a-9723-69f9936ff470/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

subject: The battle for American journalism
body:
In over sixty years in the legal profession, I've experienced first hand the impact of both good journalism and bad journalism — mostly the latter — and just how important and rare it is to get things right.

---
# FROM THE DESK OF ALAN DERSHOWITZ
---
| | Dear Peter, In over sixty years in the legal profession, I've experienced first hand the impact of both good journalism and bad journalism — mostly the latter — and just how important and rare it is to get things right.

**In today's media landscape there are far too many publications that get things wrong.** Many are more concerned with the political implications of a story than the basic facts.

**The New York Sun is different.** It's a paper that places principle over politics and people over party.

Its in-depth coverage of the legal challenges faced by President Trump serve as a case in point.

When I defended President Trump during his first impeachment, I faced an avalanche of criticism, but few papers made sufficient effort to fully appreciate the significance of the legal arguments.

The Sun's deeply knowledgeable leadership, including its editor Seth Lipsky and associate editor A.R. Hoffman are themselves students of the Constitution. It shows in their coverage, which I find to be first rate.

It will be no secret to you that I don't agree with everything that can be found in the Sun's pages, but I can say with confidence that its re-emergence is among the most important recent developments in American journalism.

I've been impressed by the Sun's growth so far, but I know it faces an uphill battle, one that I have pledged to join.

**The Sun's success will be a gift to the American people and to the cause of truth.** But to succeed, we need you to join us. I highly encourage you to join the Sun today.

Every person matters in this effort.

Don't wait. The battle to restore American journalism starts now.
---

| **Alan Dershowitz**

clean_body: In over sixty years in the legal profession, I've experienced first hand the impact of both good journalism and bad journalism — mostly the latter — and just how important and rare it is to get things right. For a limited time, join for just 1¢. | | | | FROM THE DESK OF ALAN DERSHOWITZ | | Dear Peter, In over sixty years in the legal profession, I've experienced first hand the impact of both good journalism and bad journalism — mostly the latter — and just how important and rare it is to get things right. In today's media landscape there are far too many publications that get things wrong. Many are more concerned with the political implications of a story than the basic facts. The New York Sun is different. It's a paper that places principle over politics and people over party. Its in-depth coverage of the legal challenges faced by President Trump serve as a case in point. When I defended President Trump during his first impeachment, I faced an avalanche of criticism, but few papers made sufficient effort to fully appreciate the significance of the legal arguments. The Sun's deeply knowledgeable leadership, including its editor Seth Lipsky and associate editor A.R. Hoffman are themselves students of the Constitution. It shows in their coverage, which I find to be first rate. It will be no secret to you that I don't agree with everything that can be found in the Sun's pages, but I can say with confidence that its re-emergence is among the most important recent developments in American journalism. I've been impressed by the Sun's growth so far, but I know it faces an uphill battle, one that I have pledged to join. The Sun's success will be a gift to the American people and to the cause of truth. But to succeed, we need you to join us. I highly encourage you to join the Sun today. Every person matters in this effort. Don't wait. The battle to restore American journalism starts now. | | | | | Alan Dershowitz Columnist for The New York Sun | | | | | Alan Dershowitz Columnist for The New York Sun | | |

¡Ay, Dios mio!

You won't believe this!

_**Official news just broke that Democrats are recruiting an _elitist celebrity_ to challenge me for the most important House seat in America.**_

My name is Monica De La Cruz. I'm the proud America First Hispanic conservative who humiliated Biden and the Democrats by flipping a solid blue seat in South Texas for the first time in 100 years.

By winning this race in 2024, we proved to the Left that Hispanic Americans no longer buy into their racist narrative that people who look and sound like us have to be Democrats.

We recognized that what they've done to this country is unforgivable, and we rejected them wholeheartedly by electing America First Republicans up and down the ballot.

But Democrats know how slim our GOP House majority is, and they know that my seat is the lynchpin that's keeping Republicans in charge in Washington.

_**So they 're already gearing up to challenge me in 2026.**_

And unlike the celebrity they've recruited to run against me, my campaign is fueled by grassroots support from patriots like you. **[So I'm humbly asking if you'll stand with me today by chipping in any amount.](http://links.conservativeum.com/u/click?_t=26c2e4aa9bbe40418f61e26972138f21&_m=5a36338cb3d74140bbc3e391f0f5f3f4&_e=fcIVJwgGQqMwhongNVY3kd_-yg9Yt7Sr16yTbiAYdyfYuJenDksSWAvzfVJNPWt6IZdm9x_4RQBg11xaJgKYI-_DvVFi7a37_D5_yz-rhRMPBqQPSf2ErDDTMaki1KHWbmIw7z34Z7vg2spZBEmPKRxx8A1KRCyjg0AULz1i_wExPXAsGPT49zXlGFgPayEFVaZYAxS0AgxcXOaUW1MouEjAGOF-NtF_rGFR4apIr1d9EW0NC89w0PLyeww58bcleFxVkHhFDw2mqUwDAXHQRabyhKBqjXAV3jdBw5QiPS5_13N2G4gko6gP4XsPL7oqKu49cWf28C15ykeUowv-80Vbv38tcPvb7_Ln_DU-4bfp5WYtYVQgIFkGh8BQV0EkSsj-zO6FgjZWWHYZatW_JEfbPIAfosDFbEg8ss_2xP1xeaEksjwC5cj4JRMv68ptUu8WDj_VfSEWd_mMgSrEbCh88jxvC9T820bV7_VH2nAymV83LpuFAuaw8g6Cr_dn0qAO1nbulnvcaVUvd8IbfbiavVPEx7Ox6fImX0bRbrs%3D "So I’m humbly asking if you’ll stand with me today by chipping in any amount.")**
| | [CONTRIBUTE ANY AMOUNT](http://links.conservativeum.com/u/click?_t=26c2e4aa9bbe40418f61e26972138f21&_m=5a36338cb3d74140bbc3e391f0f5f3f4&_e=fcIVJwgGQqMwhongNVY3kd_-yg9Yt7Sr16yTbiAYdyfYuJenDksSWAvzfVJNPWt6IZdm9x_4RQBg11xaJgKYI-_DvVFi7a37_D5_yz-rhRMPBqQPSf2ErDDTMaki1KHWbmIw7z34Z7vg2spZBEmPKRxx8A1KRCyjg0AULz1i_wExPXAsGPT49zXlGFgPayEFVaZYAxS0AgxcXOaUW1MouEjAGOF-NtF_rGFR4apIr1d9EW0NC89w0PLyeww58bcleFxVkHhFDw2mqUwDAXHQRabyhKBqjXAV3jdBw5QiPS5_13N2G4gko6gP4XsPL7oqKu49cWf28C15ykeUowv-80Vbv38tcPvb7_Ln_DU-4bfp5WYtYVQgIFkGh8BQV0EkSsj-zO6FgjZWWHYZatW_JFkGI836gOoClVI6ILrA3IOTAm_uU7KUTTADtYAmqJshpU8ospf0oSMMZ36Y9dXHL_KJTAN8FtlHtY3hmTZAkTnH_T85qUthVHJjA3dwO7U8H2HiwApUVe2Gr233lW82X4TQFd2oI5bUEx0rTiLsWHY%3D)
---

As the granddaughter of Mexican immigrants, I've had to work for everything I have, and I hate asking for money.

But when it comes to saving America and protecting the forgotten men and women of this country, there isn't anything I won't do.

If you stand with me today, I promise we'll keep delivering on the mandate YOU entrusted us with to turn this country around.

We can't take our foot off the gas now. Not when Democrats are working around the clock to stifle our progress.

Don't let them win. Stand with me today and help us make history once more.
| | [CONTRIBUTE ANY AMOUNT](http://links.conservativeum.com/u/click?_t=26c2e4aa9bbe40418f61e26972138f21&_m=5a36338cb3d74140bbc3e391f0f5f3f4&_e=fcIVJwgGQqMwhongNVY3kd_-yg9Yt7Sr16yTbiAYdyfYuJenDksSWAvzfVJNPWt6IZdm9x_4RQBg11xaJgKYI-_DvVFi7a37_D5_yz-rhRMPBqQPSf2ErDDTMaki1KHWbmIw7z34Z7vg2spZBEmPKRxx8A1KRCyjg0AULz1i_wExPXAsGPT49zXlGFgPayEFVaZYAxS0AgxcXOaUW1MouEjAGOF-NtF_rGFR4apIr1d9EW0NC89w0PLyeww58bcleFxVkHhFDw2mqUwDAXHQRabyhKBqjXAV3jdBw5QiPS5_13N2G4gko6gP4XsPL7oqKu49cWf28C15ykeUowv-80Vbv38tcPvb7_Ln_DU-4bfp5WYtYVQgIFkGh8BQV0EkSsj-zO6FgjZWWHYZatW_JFTyOkmdIRMphvxVNuXXAPkfm0QqMHBWMvd3VFprt-hvYJpYXfvOy5qYNGifx1AKjOKX7nb8cHelA-GwWqA0I1HTSM6Vk4E0ddwEwJgLWw0Naxc3b5NLe2WSrEXTY4iXPpm_nydcqtX7lh3hWDsn64k%3D)
---

Thank you and God bless,

_Monica De La Cruz_

clean_body: | | | | | | | | ¡Ay, Dios mio! You won't believe this! Official news just broke that Democrats are recruiting an elitist celebrity to challenge me for the most important House seat in America. My name is Monica De La Cruz. I'm the proud America First Hispanic conservative who humiliated Biden and the Democrats by flipping a solid blue seat in South Texas for the first time in 100 years. By winning this race in 2024, we proved to the Left that Hispanic Americans no longer buy into their racist narrative that people who look and sound like us have to be Democrats. We recognized that what they've done to this country is unforgivable, and we rejected them wholeheartedly by electing America First Republicans up and down the ballot. But Democrats know how slim our GOP House majority is, and they know that my seat is the lynchpin that's keeping Republicans in charge in Washington. So they 're already gearing up to challenge me in 2026. And unlike the celebrity they've recruited to run against me, my campaign is fueled by grassroots support from patriots like you. "So I’m humbly asking if you’ll stand with me today by chipping in any amount." | | As the granddaughter of Mexican immigrants, I've had to work for everything I have, and I hate asking for money. But when it comes to saving America and protecting the forgotten men and women of this country, there isn't anything I won't do. If you stand with me today, I promise we'll keep delivering on the mandate YOU entrusted us with to turn this country around. We can't take our foot off the gas now. Not when Democrats are working around the clock to stifle our progress. Don't let them win. Stand with me today and help us make history once more. | | Thank you and God bless, Monica De La Cruz | | To ensure that you don't miss out on important messages, please add info@email.monicaforcongress.org to your address book. Not Sure How? Follow the link below to your email provider to get step by step instructions on how to add us to your address book. , , , , | Paid for by Monica for Congress PO Box 4605 McAllen, Texas 78502 https:/// |

body:
Republicans control the House of Representatives by a RAZOR-THIN margin. I’m the cavalry coming in to grow our Majority.
And I’ll continue Matt Gaetz’s legacy in FL-01. Early voting is about to begin here so I won’t beat around the bush with you:
| |
---
| | | I love President Trump - I'm humbled to have earned his endorsement.

I love the Sunshine State - I'm honored to serve as Florida's CFO and State Fire Marshal.

My name is Jimmy Patronis. **I 'm Trump's guy running in the Special Election to fill Matt Gaetz's seat in Congress.**

Early voting is about to begin here so I won't beat around the bush with you:

Republicans control the House of Representatives by a RAZOR-THIN margin. I'm the cavalry coming in to grow our Majority. But I can't fill Matt Gaetz's seat without some more help.

**_[That's why I'm partnering with House Majority Leader Steve Scalise to raise $50,000 - before my all-important Special Election - to help win key races like this.](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=cbe3487d641a4fc589e6c804eea4a108&_e=v3G2GxP3aKgQ5V-I3AJO5xciZcLNEmBggCsP7Z9gw8paXdh_3ORomktv7M_3C1qokzj2WHX9VAogFtTJJDjaDz5vFGTnvAg2X2XlUXn2Vej7hdsK40gRC8DjO7EKQgrYqfWZI7483I_BYnZ_hn6IZkFCYzoFwICJTzA4mMtbYFHyeUVSFIi4cpa82MwSsEGQNTEKFh3FJE24HRSi6FekGwjA8cLaq_V0M_lPtVN6vbtw-Fg-SEsQiICW21wstxjKrMHBqE68n9IZUxIsjIX3LP2Sj6_XJUav3Vo9SRBo2rd0A_STq59wyNpPyMG-IG4TePILysHsC9rSTEJP7NQunNlrmaYm3Nu9DAs1ojeiLgvjwTcAb8IP48e_7biaOf_TIKq6PHSjtHABvR3Rd4nJJg%3D%3D "That’s why I’m partnering with House Majority Leader Steve Scalise to raise $50,000 – before my all-important Special Election – to help win key races like this.")_**

_So, if you can, I respectfully ask you to pitch in here: _

Look,

The timeline here is tight. Governor Ron DeSantis immediately called this Special Election so we could quickly fill this seat with an America First Conservative.

It's a perfect plan to help ensure we deliver on President Trump's agenda ASAP.

**But that means EVERY SECOND counts between now and the Primary in a few short weeks.**

Steve Scalise and I are partnering up today so Republicans are perfectly positioned to win this seat for President Trump. If that's what you want too, I believe I'm the right man for the job.

I'd be honored to have your support right now.

To victory,
_- Jimmy_
